Name: Bulbasaur
Bio: While it is young, it uses the nutrients that are stored in the seed on its back in order to grow.
Fact: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. The plant sprouts and grows with this Pokémon.

Name: Ivysaur
Bio: Exposure to sunlight adds to its strength. Sunlight also makes the bud on its back grow larger.
Fact: When the bulb on its back grows large, it appears to lose the ability to stand on its hind legs.

Name: Venusaur
Bio: A bewitching aroma wafts from its flower. The fragrance becalms those engaged in a battle.
Fact: The plant blooms when it is absorbing solar energy. It stays on the move to seek sunlight.

Name: Charmander
Bio: From the time it is born, a flame burns at the tip of its tail. Its life would end if the flame were to go out.
Fact: Obviously prefers hot places. When it rains, steam is said to spout from the tip of its tail.

Name: Charmeleon
Bio: If it becomes agitated during battle, it spouts intense flames, incinerating its surroundings.
Fact: When it swings its burning tail, it elevates the temperature to unbearably high levels.

Name: Charizard
Bio: Its wings can carry this Pokémon close to an altitude of 4,600 feet. It blows out fire at very high temperatures.
Fact: Spits fire that is hot enough to melt boulders. Known to cause forest fires unintentionally.

Name: Squirtle
Bio: When it feels threatened, it draws its limbs inside its shell and sprays water from its mouth.
Fact: After birth, its back swells and hardens into a shell. Powerfully sprays foam from its mouth.

Name: Wartortle
Bio: It cleverly controls its furry ears and tail to maintain its balance while swimming.
Fact: Often hides in water to stalk unwary prey. For swimming fast, it moves its ears to maintain balance.

Name: Blastoise
Bio: The rocket cannons on its shell fire jets of water capable of punching holes through thick steel.
Fact: A brutal Pokémon with pressurized water jets on its shell. They are used for high speed tackles.

Name: Caterpie
Bio: Its short feet are tipped with suction pads that enable it to tirelessly climb slopes and walls.
Fact: If you touch the feeler on top of its head, it will release a horrible stink to protect itself.

Name: Metapod
Bio: Even though it is encased in a sturdy shell, the body inside is tender. It can’t withstand a harsh attack.
Fact: This Pokémon is vulnerable to attack while its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

Name: Butterfree
Bio: It collects honey every day. It rubs honey onto the hairs on its legs to carry it back to its nest.
Fact: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

Name: Weedle
Bio: Beware of the sharp stinger on its head. It hides in grass and bushes where it eats leaves.
Fact: Often found in forests, eating leaves. It has a sharp venomous stinger on its head.

Name: Kakuna
Bio: Able to move only slightly. When endangered, it may stick out its stinger and poison its enemy.
Fact: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

Name: Beedrill
Bio: It has three poisonous stingers on its forelegs and its tail. They are used to jab its enemy repeatedly.
Fact: Flies at high speed and attacks using its large venomous stingers on its forelegs and tail.

Name: Pidgey
Bio: Very docile. If attacked, it will often kick up sand to protect itself rather than fight back.
Fact: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

Name: Pidgeotto
Bio: This Pokémon is full of vitality. It constantly flies around its large territory in search of prey.
Fact: Very protective of its sprawling territorial area, this Pokémon will fiercely peck at any intruder.

Name: Pidgeot
Bio: This Pokémon flies at Mach 2 speed, seeking prey. Its large talons are feared as wicked weapons.
Fact: When hunting, it skims the surface of water at high speed to pick off unwary prey such as MAGIKARP.

Name: Rattata
Bio: Will chew on anything with its fangs. If you see one, you can be certain that 40 more live in the area.
Fact: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

Name: Raticate
Bio: Its hind feet are webbed. They act as flippers, so it can swim in rivers and hunt for prey.
Fact: It uses its whis­ kers to maintain its balance. It apparently slows down if they are cut off.

Name: Spearow
Bio: Inept at flying high. However, it can fly around very fast to protect its territory.
Fact: Eats bugs in grassy areas. It has to flap its short wings at high speed to stay airborne.

Name: Fearow
Bio: A Pokémon that dates back many years. If it senses danger, it flies high and away, instantly.
Fact: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

Name: Ekans
Bio: The older it gets, the longer it grows. At night, it wraps its long body around tree branches to rest.
Fact: Moves silently and stealthily. Eats the eggs of birds, such as PIDGEY and SPEAROW, whole.

Name: Arbok
Bio: The frightening patterns on its belly have been studied. Six variations have been confirmed.
Fact: It is rumored that the ferocious warning markings on its belly differ from area to area.

Name: Pikachu
Bio: Possesses cheek sacs in which it stores electricity. This clever forest-dweller roasts tough berries with an electric shock before consuming them.
Fact: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity could build and cause lightning storms.

Name: Raichu
Bio: It can discharge bursts of electricity exceeding 100,000 volts— a single strike with that amount of power would incapacitate one of the Copperajah of my homeland.
Fact: Its long tail serves as a ground to protect itself from its own high voltage power.

Name: Sandshrew
Bio: It burrows into the ground to create its nest. If hard stones impede its tunneling, it uses its sharp claws to shatter them and then carries on digging.
Fact: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

Name: Sandslash
Bio: It climbs trees by hooking on with its sharp claws. Sandslash shares the berries it gathers, dropping them down to Sandshrew waiting below the tree.
Fact: Curls up into a spiny ball when threatened. It can roll while curled up to attack or escape.

Name: Nidoran♀
Bio: It uses its hard incisor teeth to crush and eat berries. The tip of a female Nidoran’s horn is a bit more rounded than the tip of a male’s horn.
Fact: Although small, its venomous barbs render this Pokémon dangerous. The female has smaller horns.

Name: Nidorina
Bio: If the group is threatened, these Pokémon will band together to assault enemies with a chorus of ultrasonic waves.
Fact: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

Name: Nidoqueen
Bio: It pacifies offspring by placing them in the gaps between the spines on its back. The spines will never secrete poison while young are present.
Fact: Its hard scales provide strong protection. It uses its hefty bulk to execute powerful moves.

Name: Nidoran♂
Bio: Small but brave, this Pokémon will hold its ground and even risk its life in battle to protect the female it’s friendly with.
Fact: Stiffens its ears to sense danger. The larger its horns, the more powerful its secreted venom.

Name: Nidorino
Bio: It’s nervous and quick to act aggressively. The potency of its poison increases along with the level of adrenaline present in its body.
Fact: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

Name: Nidoking
Bio: Nidoking prides itself on its strength. It’s forceful and spirited in battle, making use of its thick tail and diamond-crushing horn.
Fact: It uses its powerful tail in battle to smash, constrict, then break the prey's bones.

Name: Clefairy
Bio: It can be found in quiet mountain areas on a full moon's night. Its dancing and its tiny, faintly glowing wings confer upon it a lovely fairylike quality.
Fact: Its magical and cute appeal has many admirers. It is rare and found only in certain areas.

Name: Clefable
Bio: Legend says that on clear, quiet nights, it listens for the voices of its kin living on the moon. I, too, often think of my homeland, so far away.
Fact: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen. It will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Vulpix
Bio: In its belly burns a fire, which Vulpix spits out in the form of fireballs. When young, this Pokémon has but one white tail. As the Pokémon matures, this single tail splits into six.
Fact: At the time of birth, it has just one tail. The tail splits from its tip as it grows older.

Name: Ninetales
Bio: The coat of gleaming golden fur is quite magnificent. This species is said to store sacred power in its nine long tails and to live for a millennium.
Fact: Very smart and very vengeful. Grabbing one of its many tails could result in a 1000-year curse.

Name: Jigglypuff
Bio: By freely changing the wavelength of its voice, Jigglypuff sings a mysterious melody sure to make any listener sleepy.
Fact: When its huge eyes light up, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

Name: Wigglytuff
Bio: It’s proud of its fur, which is fine and delicate. In particular, the curl on its forehead has a texture that’s perfectly heavenly.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (140) of all 52 Fairy-type Pokémon.

Name: Zubat
Bio: Makes its home in gloomy caves. Atrophied eyes have left this Pokémon blind, so it scans its surroundings via sound waves that it emits from its mouth as it flies.
Fact: Forms colonies in perpetually dark places. Uses ultrasonic waves to identify and approach targets.

Name: Golbat
Bio: It sinks its sharp fangs into other creatures and slurps up their blood. A closer look at the fangs reveals that they are hollow and akin to straws.
Fact: Once it strikes, it will not stop draining energy from the victim even if it gets too heavy to fly.

Name: Oddish
Bio: During the day, it stays in the cold underground to avoid the sun. It grows by bathing in moonlight.
Fact: During the day, it keeps its face buried in the ground. At night, it wanders around sowing its seeds.

Name: Gloom
Bio: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.
Fact: The fluid that oozes from its mouth isn't drool. It is a nectar that is used to attract prey.

Name: Vileplume
Bio: The larger its petals, the more toxic pollen it contains. Its big head is heavy and hard to hold up.
Fact: Flaps its broad flower petals to scatter its poisonous pollen. The flapping sound is very loud.

Name: Paras
Bio: Sometimes seen at the foot of trees in humid forests. The mushrooms on its back—called tochukaso—are not present on infant specimens and instead emerge as Paras matures.
Fact: Burrows to suck tree roots. The mushrooms on its back grow by draw­ ing nutrients from the bug host.

Name: Parasect
Bio: Mushroom-lacking specimens of this Pokémon lie unmoving in the forest, lending credence to the hypothesis that the large mushroom is in control of Parasect's actions.
Fact: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

Name: Venonat
Bio: Its large eyes act as radar. In a bright place, you can see that they are clusters of many tiny eyes.
Fact: Lives in the shadows of tall trees where it eats insects. It is attracted by light at night.

Name: Venomoth
Bio: The powdery scales on its wings are hard to remove from skin. They also contain poison that leaks out on contact.
Fact: The dust-like scales covering its wings are color coded to indicate the kinds of poison it has.

Name: Diglett
Bio: It burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.
Fact: Lives about one yard underground where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears above ground.

Name: Dugtrio
Bio: These Diglett triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it’s like underground.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (120) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Meowth
Bio: It washes its face regularly to keep the coin on its forehead spotless. It doesn’t get along with Galarian Meowth.
Fact: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

Name: Persian
Bio: Its elegant and refined behavior clashes with that of the barbaric Perrserker. The relationship between the two is one of mutual disdain.
Fact: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

Name: Psyduck
Bio: Suffers perpetual headaches. If the agony grows too great, Psyduck's latent power erupts, contrary to Psyduck's intent. Ergo, I am exploring ways to ease the pain.
Fact: While lulling its enemies with its vacant look, this wily Pokémon will use psychokinetic powers.

Name: Golduck
Bio: Its body is strong, and it has webbing on its hands and feet. Golduck can swim easily through rough seas, clawing its way through the high waves.
Fact: Often seen swim­ ming elegantly by lake shores. It is often mistaken for the Japanese monster, Kappa.

Name: Mankey
Bio: An agile Pokémon that lives in trees. It angers easily and will not hesitate to attack anything.
Fact: Extremely quick to anger. It could be docile one moment then thrashing away the next instant.

Name: Primeape
Bio: It stops being angry only when nobody else is around. To view this moment is very difficult.
Fact: Always furious and tenacious to boot. It will not abandon chasing its quarry until it is caught.

Name: Growlithe
Bio: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.
Fact: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

Name: Arcanine
Bio: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.
Fact: A Pokémon that has been admired since the past for its beauty. It runs agilely as if on wings.

Name: Poliwag
Bio: In rivers with fast-flowing water, this Pokémon will cling to a rock by using its thick lips, which act like a suction cup.
Fact: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming than trying to stand.

Name: Poliwhirl
Bio: This Pokémon’s sweat is a slimy mucus. When captured, Poliwhirl can slither from its enemies’ grasp and escape.
Fact: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

Name: Poliwrath
Bio: Poliwrath is skilled at both swimming and martial arts. It uses its well-trained arms to dish out powerful punches.
Fact: An adept swimmer at both the front crawl and breast stroke. Easily overtakes the best human swimmers.

Name: Abra
Bio: Spends 18 hours of the day sleeping. Even while asleep, Abra can control its psychic powers—should danger approach, the Pokémon will simply teleport away.
Fact: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and TELEPORT to safety.

Name: Kadabra
Bio: There are rumors that a child with mystical powers became a Kadabra; however, this remains unverified. I suspect that the spoon Kadabra holds enhances its brain waves.
Fact: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches just by being close by.

Name: Alakazam
Bio: The longer Alakazam lives, the larger and heavier its head becomes. Our tests have shown that the strength of its psychic powers correlates positively to the weight of its head.
Fact: Its brain can out­ perform a super­ computer. Its intelligence quotient is said to be 5,000.

Name: Machop
Bio: Though as small as a child, it has strength enough to easily throw a well-built adult. Striving to become ever stronger, Machop trains by carrying a Graveler on its shoulders.
Fact: Loves to build its muscles. It trains in all styles of martial arts to become even stronger.

Name: Machoke
Bio: A sturdy creature boasting a robust physique and boundless stamina. Loves training above all else and voluntarily assists with tasks such as construction and clearing land.
Fact: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

Name: Machamp
Bio: In close combat, its four arms afford it offensive and defensive supremacy. In but a blink, this valiant Pokémon can overwhelm its foes with more than 1,000 blows from its fists.
Fact: Using its heavy muscles, it throws powerful punches that can send the victim clear over the horizon.

Name: Bellsprout
Bio: Prefers hot and humid places. It ensnares tiny bugs with its vines and devours them.
Fact: A carnivorous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

Name: Weepinbell
Bio: When hungry, it swallows anything that moves. Its hapless prey is dissolved by strong acids.
Fact: It spits out POISONPOWDER to immobilize the enemy and then finishes it with a spray of ACID.

Name: Victreebel
Bio: Lures prey with the sweet aroma of honey. Swallowed whole, the prey is dissolved in a day, bones and all.
Fact: Said to live in huge colonies deep in jungles, although no one has ever returned from there.

Name: Tentacool
Bio: They fire beams from the glassy, magenta orbs that resemble eyes atop their heads, and they drift in shallow seas. During low tide, they can sometimes be found on beaches, desiccated.
Fact: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.

Name: Tentacruel
Bio: It has 80 tentacles, each with a venomous tip. These tentacles are also extendible, lengthening when Tentacruel attempts to catch prey. Use caution.
Fact: The tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, they are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.

Name: Geodude
Bio: Makes its home in mountainous regions, using its arms to climb along harsh mountain roads. Can be troublesome—carelessly kicking one will cause it to fly into a rage and chase after you.
Fact: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

Name: Graveler
Bio: Dwells in holes dug into sheer walls of stone. It enjoys rolling down slopes as though it were a boulder during a rockfall, so keep an eye upward while traversing mountain roads.
Fact: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

Name: Golem
Bio: The rocklike shell is shed each year. The cast-off shell then crumbles, reverting to a mass of soil, which can be spread across fields to promote crop growth.
Fact: Its boulder-like body is extremely hard. It can easily withstand dynamite blasts without damage.

Name: Ponyta
Bio: These Pokémon live in herds out in the grassland. Newborn foals lack their fiery manes, which will develop about an hour after birth.
Fact: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything completely flat in little time.

Name: Rapidash
Bio: Fiery mane aglow, Rapidash darts like an arrow across the land. This prodigiously swift creature can traverse the vast region of Hisui in a day and a half.
Fact: Very competitive, this Pokémon will chase anything that moves fast in the hopes of racing it.

Name: Slowpoke
Bio: When this Pokémon’s tail is soaked in water, sweetness seeps from it. Slowpoke uses this trait to lure in and fish up other Pokémon.
Fact: Incredibly slow and dopey. It takes 5 seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.

Name: Slowbro
Bio: Being bitten by a Shellder shocked this Pokémon into standing on two legs. If the Shellder lets go, it seems Slowbro will turn back into a Slowpoke.
Fact: The SHELLDER that is latched onto SLOWPOKE's tail is said to feed on the host's left over scraps.

Name: Magnemite
Bio: A bizarre Pokémon with but a single eye embedded in an iron sphere. I suspect this creature levitates due to the magnetism it emits from its arms, which resemble horseshoe-shaped magnets.
Fact: Uses anti-gravity to stay suspended. Appears without warning and uses THUNDER WAVE and similar moves.

Name: Magneton
Bio: Three Magnemite gathered to evolve into this Pokémon. The source of much vexation on my part, as its powerful magnetism destroys my research equipment.
Fact: Formed by several MAGNEMITEs linked together. They frequently appear when sunspots flare up.

Name: Farfetch'd
Bio: They use a plant stalk as a weapon, but not all of them use it in the same way. Several distinct styles of stalk fighting have been observed.
Fact: The sprig of green onions it holds is its weapon. It is used much like a metal sword.

Name: Doduo
Bio: Its short wings make flying difficult. Instead, this Pokémon runs at high speed on developed legs.
Fact: A bird that makes up for its poor flying with its fast foot speed. Leaves giant footprints.

Name: Dodrio
Bio: One of Doduo’s two heads splits to form a unique species. It runs close to 40 mph in prairies.
Fact: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

Name: Seel
Bio: Loves freezing-cold conditions. Relishes swimming in a frigid climate of around 14 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. It is used for bashing through thick ice.

Name: Dewgong
Bio: Its entire body is a snowy white. Unharmed by even intense cold, it swims powerfully in icy waters.
Fact: Stores thermal energy in its body. Swims at a steady 8 knots even in intensely cold waters.

Name: Grimer
Bio: Made of congealed sludge. It smells too putrid to touch. Even weeds won’t grow in its path.
Fact: Appears in filthy areas. Thrives by sucking up polluted sludge that is pumped out of factories.

Name: Muk
Bio: Smells so awful, it can cause fainting. Through degeneration of its nose, it lost its sense of smell.
Fact: Thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.

Name: Shellder
Bio: Its hard shell repels any kind of attack. It is vulnerable only when its shell is open.
Fact: The shell can withstand any attack. However, when it is open, the tender body is exposed.

Name: Cloyster
Bio: Once it slams its shell shut, it is impossible to open, even by those with superior strength.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (180) of all 141 Water-type Pokémon.

Name: Gastly
Bio: Gaseous and completely impalpable. Also highly dangerous— inhaling part of its poisonous body will cause one to faint instantly.
Fact: Tied for the lightest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 kg.

Name: Haunter
Bio: This frightful, malevolent spirit can glide through walls, appearing wherever it likes. According to rumor, victims of a Haunter's lick will wither to death day by day.
Fact: Tied for the lightest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 kg.

Name: Gengar
Bio: Possesses potential victims' shadows in an effort to steal away the victims' lives. If your shadow begins to laugh, you must take hold of a protective charm posthaste!
Fact: Under a full moon, this Pokémon likes to mimic the shadows of people and laugh at their fright.

Name: Onix
Bio: This chain of immense stones resembles a giant serpent. Tremors shake the earth above as it burrows deep beneath the ground, feeding on boulders as it goes.
Fact: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, but colored black.

Name: Drowzee
Bio: If you sleep by it all the time, it will sometimes show you dreams it had eaten in the past.
Fact: Puts enemies to sleep then eats their dreams. Occasionally gets sick from eating bad dreams.

Name: Hypno
Bio: Avoid eye contact if you come across one. It will try to put you to sleep by using its pendulum.
Fact: When it locks eyes with an enemy, it will use a mix of PSI moves such as HYPNOSIS and CONFUSION.

Name: Krabby
Bio: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.
Fact: Its pincers are not only powerful weapons, they are used for balance when walking sideways.

Name: Kingler
Bio: Its oversized claw is very powerful, but when it’s not in battle, the claw just gets in the way.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (130) of all 141 Water-type Pokémon.

Name: Voltorb
Bio: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.
Fact: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a POKé BALL, they have zapped many people.

Name: Electrode
Bio: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.
Fact: It stores electric energy under very high pressure. It often explodes with little or no provocation.

Name: Exeggcute
Bio: These Pokémon get nervous when they’re not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.
Fact: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

Name: Exeggutor
Bio: When they work together, Exeggutor’s three heads can put out powerful psychic energy. Cloudy days make this Pokémon sluggish.
Fact: Legend has it that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an EXEGGCUTE.

Name: Cubone
Bio: This Pokémon wears the skull of its deceased mother. Sometimes Cubone’s dreams make it cry, but each tear Cubone sheds makes it stronger.
Fact: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.

Name: Marowak
Bio: When this Pokémon evolved, the skull of its mother fused to it. Marowak’s temperament also turned vicious at the same time.
Fact: The bone it holds is its key weapon. It throws the bone skillfully like a boomerang to KO targets.

Name: Hitmonlee
Bio: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.
Fact: When in a hurry, its legs lengthen progressively. It runs smoothly with extra long, loping strides.

Name: Hitmonchan
Bio: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.
Fact: While apparently doing nothing, it fires punches in lightning fast volleys that are impossible to see.

Name: Lickitung
Bio: Wields its long tongue deftly, as though it were an arm. The Pokémon's viscous saliva, once it has been collected and boiled down, yields a strong and highly useful adhesive.
Fact: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a tingling sensation when it licks enemies.

Name: Koffing
Bio: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.
Fact: Because it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.

Name: Weezing
Bio: It can’t suck in air quite as well as a Galarian Weezing, but the toxins it creates are more potent than those of its counterpart.
Fact: Where two kinds of poison gases meet, 2 KOFFINGs can fuse into a WEEZING over many years.

Name: Rhyhorn
Bio: Ludicrously strong—when it butts heads with a mountain, it is the mountain that shatters. But its short legs struggle with turns, and it is incapable of stopping unless it collides with something.
Fact: Its massive bones are 1000 times harder than human bones. It can easily knock a trailer flying.

Name: Rhydon
Bio: Rapidly rotates its horn to bore through bedrock. It swaggers around volcanic regions, protected from the lava's heat by its tough, armorlike hide.
Fact: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees.

Name: Chansey
Bio: This purehearted Pokémon shares its eggs with the injured.
Fact: A rare and elusive Pokémon that is said to bring happiness to those who manage to get it.

Name: Tangela
Bio: It is cloaked entirely in blue vines, preventing any glimpse of its true identity. The vines impart a refreshing sensation when chewed—they're useful as a spice.
Fact: The whole body is swathed with wide vines that are similar to sea­ weed. Its vines shake as it walks.

Name: Kangaskhan
Bio: There are records of a lost human child being raised by a childless Kangaskhan.
Fact: The infant rarely ventures out of its mother's protective pouch until it is 3 years old.

Name: Horsea
Bio: They swim with dance-like motions and cause whirlpools to form. Horsea compete to see which of them can generate the biggest whirlpool.
Fact: Known to shoot down flying bugs with precision blasts of ink from the surface of the water.

Name: Seadra
Bio: Seadra’s mouth is slender, but its suction power is strong. In an instant, Seadra can suck in food that’s larger than the opening of its mouth.
Fact: Capable of swim­ ming backwards by rapidly flapping its wing-like pectoral fins and stout tail.

Name: Goldeen
Bio: Its dorsal and pectoral fins are strongly developed like muscles. It can swim at a speed of five knots.
Fact: Its tail fin billows like an elegant ballroom dress, giving it the nickname of the Water Queen.

Name: Seaking
Bio: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.
Fact: In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming power­ fully up rivers and creeks.

Name: Staryu
Bio: Fish Pokémon nibble at it, but Staryu isn’t bothered. Its body regenerates quickly, even if part of it is completely torn off.
Fact: An enigmatic Pokémon that can effortlessly regenerate any appendage it loses in battle.

Name: Starmie
Bio: Starmie swims by spinning its body at high speed. As this Pokémon cruises through the ocean, it absorbs tiny plankton.
Fact: Its central core glows with the seven colors of the rainbow. Some people value the core as a gem.

Name: Mr. Mime
Bio: The behavior of this clown-like Pokémon reminds one of pantomime. It creates invisible walls using a force emitted from its fingertips.
Fact: If interrupted while it is miming, it will slap around the offender with its broad hands.

Name: Scyther
Bio: The large, wickedly sharp scythes on its forearms are truly fearsome weapons. Prey's attempts to flee are unfailingly thwarted by this Pokémon's nimble motions.
Fact: With ninja-like agility and speed, it can create the illusion that there is more than one.

Name: Jynx
Bio: The Jynx of Galar often have beautiful and delicate voices. Some of these Pokémon have even gathered a fan base.
Fact: It seductively wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

Name: Electabuzz
Bio: Feeds on electrical energy. During sudden showers beneath looming thunderclouds, one can observe Electabuzz scaling tall trees, where the Pokémon will then wait for lightning to strike.
Fact: Normally found near power plants, they can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

Name: Magmar
Bio: Legend has it that this Pokémon was born from the crater of a volcano. When wounded, it bathes in lava to heal its body, much as one would soak in a hot spring.
Fact: Its body always burns with an orange glow that enables it to hide perfectly among flames.

Name: Pinsir
Bio: This Pokémon clamps its pincers down on its prey and then either splits the prey in half or flings it away.
Fact: If it fails to crush the victim in its pincers, it will swing it around and toss it hard.

Name: Tauros
Bio: The Tauros of the Galar region are volatile in nature, and they won’t allow people to ride on their backs.
Fact: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

Name: Magikarp
Bio: A feeble, pitiful imbecile of a Pokémon that is nonetheless very hardy. Unperturbed by turbid water, it can be found living in all sorts of places.
Fact: In the distant past, it was somewhat stronger than the horribly weak descendants that exist today.

Name: Gyarados
Bio: I suspect this Pokémon to be the true identity of a dragon written of in ancient texts, which claimed that it razed an entire village with white-hot beams from its maw.
Fact: Rarely seen in the wild. Huge and vicious, it is capable of destroying entire cities in a rage.

Name: Lapras
Bio: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (130) of all 41 Ice-type Pokémon.

Name: Ditto
Bio: When it encounters another Ditto, it will move faster than normal to duplicate that opponent exactly.
Fact: Capable of copying an enemy's genetic code to instantly transform itself into a duplicate of the enemy.

Name: Eevee
Bio: Harbors the potential to evolve into manifold forms. Within Eevee lies the key to the mysteries of Pokémon evolution—I'm certain of it.
Fact: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from element STONEs.

Name: Vaporeon
Bio: Tests show that its cells closely resemble water molecules, which perhaps explains its ability to conceal its form while submerged. I believe the origins of mermaid folklore lie with this Pokémon.
Fact: Lives close to water. Its long tail is ridged with a fin which is often mistaken for a mermaid's.

Name: Jolteon
Bio: Bristles its fur into sharp, needlelike points when enraged. One can hear electricity crackle in its breath when it exhales.
Fact: It accumulates negative ions in the atmosphere to blast out 10000- volt lightning bolts.

Name: Flareon
Bio: Flames burn within a saclike organ inside this Pokémon. When Flareon inhales, these flames grow in intensity, reaching a mighty 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: When storing thermal energy in its body, its temperature could soar to over 1600 degrees.

Name: Porygon
Bio: It has no discernible heartbeat and does not seem to draw breath, and yet it appears to function without issue. I cannot even begin to explain this utterly bizarre anomaly.
Fact: A Pokémon that consists entirely of programming code. Capable of moving freely in cyberspace.

Name: Omanyte
Bio: This Pokémon is a member of an ancient, extinct species. Omanyte paddles through water with its 10 tentacles, looking like it’s just drifting along.
Fact: Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.

Name: Omastar
Bio: Omastar’s sharp fangs could crush rock, but the Pokémon can attack only the prey that come within reach of its tentacles.
Fact: A prehistoric Pokémon that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible to catch prey.

Name: Kabuto
Bio: While some say this species has gone extinct, Kabuto sightings are apparently fairly common in some places.
Fact: A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil found in what was once the ocean floor eons ago.

Name: Kabutops
Bio: The cause behind the extinction of this species is unknown. Kabutops were aggressive Pokémon that inhabited warm seas.
Fact: Its sleek shape is perfect for swim­ ming. It slashes prey with its claws and drains the body fluids.

Name: Aerodactyl
Bio: Aerodactyl’s sawlike fangs can shred skin to tatters—even the skin of Steel-type Pokémon.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (130) of all 65 Rock-type Pokémon.

Name: Snorlax
Bio: This glutton appears in villages without warning and devours the entirety of their rice granaries—such occurrences have long been counted among the gravest of disasters.
Fact: Very lazy. Just eats and sleeps. As its rotund bulk builds, it becomes steadily more slothful.

Name: Articuno
Bio: This Pokémon can control ice at will. Articuno is said to live in snowy mountains riddled with permafrost.
Fact: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

Name: Zapdos
Bio: Zapdos is a legendary bird Pokémon. It’s said that when Zapdos rubs its feathers together, lightning will fall immediately after.
Fact: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear from clouds while dropping enormous lightning bolts.

Name: Moltres
Bio: There are stories of this Pokémon using its radiant, flame-cloaked wings to light up paths for those lost in the mountains.
Fact: Known as the legendary bird of fire. Every flap of its wings creates a dazzling flash of flames.

Name: Dratini
Bio: This Pokémon was long considered to be no more than a myth. The small lump on a Dratini’s forehead is actually a horn that’s still coming in.
Fact: Long considered a mythical Pokémon until recently when a small colony was found living underwater.

Name: Dragonair
Bio: This Pokémon gathers power in the orbs on its tail and controls the weather. When enshrouded by an aura, Dragonair has a mystical appearance.
Fact: A mystical Pokémon that exudes a gentle aura. Has the ability to change climate conditions.

Name: Dragonite
Bio: This Pokémon is known as the Sea Incarnate. Figureheads that resemble Dragonite decorate the bows of many ships.
Fact: An extremely rarely seen marine Pokémon. Its intelligence is said to match that of humans.

Name: Mewtwo
Bio: Its DNA is almost the same as Mew’s. However, its size and disposition are vastly different.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (154) of all 90 Psychic-type Pokémon.

Name: Mew
Bio: When viewed through a microscope, this Pokémon’s short, fine, delicate hair can be seen.
Fact: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

Name: Chikorita
Bio: In battle, Chikorita waves its leaf around to keep the foe at bay. However, a sweet fragrance also wafts from the leaf, becalming the battling Pokémon and creating a cozy, friendly atmosphere all around.
Fact: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up the sun's rays.

Name: Bayleef
Bio: Bayleef’s neck is ringed by curled-up leaves. Inside each tubular leaf is a small shoot of a tree. The fragrance of this shoot makes people peppy.
Fact: The scent of spices comes from around its neck. Somehow, sniffing it makes you want to fight.

Name: Meganium
Bio: The fragrance of Meganium’s flower soothes and calms emotions. In battle, this Pokémon gives off more of its becalming scent to blunt the foe’s fighting spirit.
Fact: The aroma that rises from its petals contains a substance that calms aggressive feelings.

Name: Cyndaquil
Bio: Hails from the Johto region. Though usually curled into a ball due to its timid disposition, it harbors tremendous firepower.
Fact: It is timid, and always curls it­ self up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

Name: Quilava
Bio: This creature's fur is most mysterious—it is wholly impervious to the burning touch of flame. Should Quilava turn its back to you, take heed! Such a posture indicates a forthcoming attack.
Fact: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

Name: Typhlosion
Bio: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui's center.
Fact: If its rage peaks, it becomes so hot that anything that touches it will instantly go up in flames.

Name: Totodile
Bio: Despite the smallness of its body, Totodile’s jaws are very powerful. While the Pokémon may think it is just playfully nipping, its bite has enough power to cause serious injury.
Fact: Its well-developed jaws are powerful and capable of crushing anything. Even its trainer must be careful.

Name: Croconaw
Bio: Once Croconaw has clamped its jaws on its foe, it will absolutely not let go. Because the tips of its fangs are forked back like barbed fishhooks, they become impossible to remove when they have sunk in.
Fact: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

Name: Feraligatr
Bio: Feraligatr intimidates its foes by opening its huge mouth. In battle, it will kick the ground hard with its thick and powerful hind legs to charge at the foe at an incredible speed.
Fact: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.

Name: Sentret
Bio: When Sentret sleeps, it does so while another stands guard. The sentry wakes the others at the first sign of danger. When this Pokémon becomes separated from its pack, it becomes incapable of sleep due to fear.
Fact: A very cautious Pokémon, it raises itself up using its tail to get a better view of its surroundings.

Name: Furret
Bio: Furret has a very slim build. When under attack, it can slickly squirm through narrow spaces and get away. In spite of its short limbs, this Pokémon is very nimble and fleet.
Fact: It makes a nest to suit its long and skinny body. The nest is impossible for other Pokémon to enter.

Name: Hoothoot
Bio: It begins to hoot at the same time every day. Some Trainers use them in place of clocks.
Fact: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

Name: Noctowl
Bio: When it needs to think, it rotates its head 180 degrees to sharpen its intellectual power.
Fact: Its eyes are specially adapted. They concentrate even faint light and enable it to see in the dark.

Name: Ledyba
Bio: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.
Fact: It is very timid. It will be afraid to move if it is alone. But it will be active if it is in a group.

Name: Ledian
Bio: It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to you, good things will happen to you.
Fact: When the stars flicker in the night sky, it flutters about, scattering a glowing powder.

Name: Spinarak
Bio: Although the poison from its fangs isn’t that strong, it’s potent enough to weaken prey that gets caught in its web.
Fact: It lies still in the same pose for days in its web, waiting for its unsuspecting prey to wander close.

Name: Ariados
Bio: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.
Fact: It spins string not only from its rear but also from its mouth. It is hard to tell which end is which.

Name: Crobat
Bio: Its hind limbs have become another set of wings. Crobat expertly maneuvers its four wings to dart in exquisite fashion through even the most confined caves without losing any speed.
Fact: It flies so si­ lently through the dark on its four wings that it may not be noticed even when nearby.

Name: Chinchou
Bio: On the dark ocean floor, its only means of communication is its constantly flashing lights.
Fact: It shoots positive and negative elec­ tricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

Name: Lanturn
Bio: This Pokémon flashes a bright light that blinds its prey. This creates an opening for it to deliver an electrical attack.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (125) of all 57 Electric-type Pokémon.

Name: Pichu
Bio: Pichu stores electricity in the sacs on its cheeks but discharges it inadvertently when agitated or excited. Being yet immature, the Pokémon's handling of electricity is rather inept.
Fact: It is not yet skilled at storing electricity. It may send out a jolt if amused or startled.

Name: Cleffa
Bio: In silhouette, they resemble twinkling starlight. When shooting stars rain from the night sky, Cleffa gather in numbers and dance as though they are indeed incarnations of the stars.
Fact: Because of its unusual, star-like silhouette, people believe that it came here on a meteor.

Name: Igglybuff
Bio: Taking advantage of the softness of its body, Igglybuff moves as if bouncing. Its body turns a deep pink when its temperature rises.
Fact: It has a very soft body. If it starts to roll, it will bounce all over and be impossible to stop.

Name: Togepi
Bio: This ovate creature is frequently mistaken for a moving egg when encountered out in the fields or in the mountains. Its guileless smile soothes the soul.
Fact: The shell seems to be filled with joy. It is said that it will share good luck when treated kindly.

Name: Togetic
Bio: No records exist of Togetic being seen in the wilds. Rumors abound that it evolves under the loving care of a trusted human companion, upon whom the Pokémon then bestows great joy.
Fact: They say that it will appear before kindhearted, car­ ing people and shower them with happiness.

Name: Natu
Bio: Because its wings aren’t yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always staring at something.
Fact: Because its wings aren't yet fully grown, it has to hop to get around. It is always star­ ing at something.

Name: Xatu
Bio: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun’s movement all day.
Fact: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

Name: Mareep
Bio: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You’ll want to pet it because it’s cute, but if you use your bare hand, you’ll get a painful shock.
Fact: If static elec­ tricity builds in its body, its fleece doubles in volume. Touching it will shock you.

Name: Flaaffy
Bio: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.
Fact: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

Name: Ampharos
Bio: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.
Fact: The tail's tip shines brightly and can be seen from far away. It acts as a beacon for lost people.

Name: Bellossom
Bio: Bellossom gather at times and appear to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.
Fact: BELLOSSOM gather at times and appear to dance. They say that the dance is a ritual to summon the sun.

Name: Marill
Bio: Even after Marill swims in a cold sea, its water- repellent fur dries almost as soon as Marill leaves the water. That’s why this Pokémon is never cold.
Fact: The tip of its tail, which con­ tains oil that is lighter than wa­ ter, lets it swim without drowning.

Name: Azumarill
Bio: These Pokémon create air-filled bubbles. When Azurill play in rivers, Azumarill will cover them with these bubbles.
Fact: By keeping still and listening in­ tently, it can tell what is in even wild, fast- moving rivers.

Name: Sudowoodo
Bio: Though it pretends to be a tree, it fails to fool even children. To the touch, its body feels more like rock than tree bark. Sudowoodo's extreme aversion to water merits special note.
Fact: Although it always pretends to be a tree, its composi­ tion appears to be closer to a rock than a plant.

Name: Politoed
Bio: The cry of a male is louder than that of a female. Male Politoed with deep, menacing voices find more popularity with the opposite gender.
Fact: If POLIWAG and POLIWHIRL hear its echoing cry, they respond by gather­ ing from far and wide.

Name: Hoppip
Bio: This Pokémon drifts and floats with the wind. If it senses the approach of strong winds, Hoppip links its leaves with other Hoppip to prepare against being blown away.
Fact: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. They do enjoy gentle breezes, though.

Name: Skiploom
Bio: Skiploom’s flower blossoms when the temperature rises above 64 degrees Fahrenheit. How much the flower opens depends on the temperature. For that reason, this Pokémon is sometimes used as a thermometer.
Fact: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluc­ tuates up and down.

Name: Jumpluff
Bio: Jumpluff rides warm southern winds to cross the sea and fly to foreign lands. The Pokémon descends to the ground when it encounters cold air while it is floating.
Fact: Once it catches the wind, it deft­ ly controls its cotton-puff spores to float, even around the world.

Name: Aipom
Bio: This treetop dweller possesses a tail as dexterous as a hand. Ancient writings describe this Pokémon as a one-armed oddity.
Fact: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

Name: Sunkern
Bio: Sunkern tries to move as little as it possibly can. It does so because it tries to conserve all the nutrients it has stored in its body for its evolution. It will not eat a thing, subsisting only on morning dew.
Fact: It may drop out of the sky suddenly. If attacked by a SPEAROW, it will violently shake its leaves.

Name: Sunflora
Bio: Sunflora converts solar energy into nutrition. It moves around actively in the daytime when it is warm. It stops moving as soon as the sun goes down for the night.
Fact: It converts sun­ light into energy. In the darkness after sunset, it closes its petals and becomes still.

Name: Yanma
Bio: Its frail wings are so thin that one can see clear through them. However, during flight these wings exhibit the power to churn air with force enough to launch a house skyward.
Fact: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter win­ dows in the area.

Name: Wooper
Bio: When walking on land, it covers its body with a poisonous film that keeps its skin from dehydrating.
Fact: This Pokémon lives in cold water. It will leave the water to search for food when it gets cold outside.

Name: Quagsire
Bio: Its body is always slimy. It often bangs its head on the river bottom as it swims but seems not to care.
Fact: This carefree Pokémon has an easy-going nature. While swimming, it always bumps into boat hulls.

Name: Espeon
Bio: Wields an arcane power with which it can predict the weather and even people's thoughts. When bathed in sunshine, the scarlet orb on its brow glows and builds energy.
Fact: It uses the fine hair that covers its body to sense air currents and predict its ene­ my's actions.

Name: Umbreon
Bio: It is most active in the wee hours of the night, when moonlight bathes the land. Its large eyes can pierce the darkness and perceive prey with absolute clarity.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (130) of all 55 Dark-type Pokémon.

Name: Murkrow
Bio: Widely shunned as a bearer of ill fortune. Upon crossing paths with this creature, I've been told one must chant ”Workrum, Workrum—bad luck, don't come” as a protective incantation.
Fact: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

Name: Slowking
Bio: Slowking can solve any problem presented to it, but no one can understand a thing Slowking says.
Fact: It has incredible intellect and in­ tuition. Whatever the situation, it remains calm and collected.

Name: Misdreavus
Bio: It conceals itself in darkness, sending chills up travelers' spines with its childlike weeping. As it observes the frightened travelers with glee, the red orbs upon its chest let off an eerie light.
Fact: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

Name: Unown
Bio: It is hard to believe these strangely shaped Pokémon are truly living creatures. I've pointed out that the species' many forms resemble writing from other lands; no one will take me seriously.
Fact: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tab­ lets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

Name: Wobbuffet
Bio: To keep its pitch-black tail hidden, it lives quietly in the darkness. It is never first to attack.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (190) of all 90 Psychic-type Pokémon.

Name: Girafarig
Bio: Girafarig’s rear head contains a tiny brain that is too small for thinking. However, the rear head doesn’t need to sleep, so it can keep watch over its surroundings 24 hours a day.
Fact: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

Name: Pineco
Bio: It sticks tree bark to itself with its saliva, making itself thicker and larger. Elderly Pineco are ridiculously huge.
Fact: It likes to make its shell thicker by adding layers of tree bark. The additional weight doesn't bother it.

Name: Forretress
Bio: In the moment that it gulps down its prey, the inside of its shell is exposed, but to this day, no one has ever seen that sight.
Fact: Its entire body is shielded by a steel-hard shell. What lurks inside the armor is a total mystery.

Name: Dunsparce
Bio: The nests Dunsparce live in are mazes of tunnels. They never get lost in their own nests—they can tell where they are by the scent of the dirt.
Fact: When spotted, this Pokémon escapes backward by furi­ ously boring into the ground with its tail.

Name: Gligar
Bio: Its tail is tipped by a thick, horrible stinger. To bring down prey, it will first obscure their vision by covering their faces with its body, and then it will use the stinger to inject them with venom.
Fact: It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the star­ tled victim to inject poison.

Name: Steelix
Bio: This Pokémon evolved through use of a strange item. Its body is coated with steel powder and notably hard—not even diamond can leave so much as a scratch.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (200) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Snubbull
Bio: In contrast to its appearance, it’s quite timid. When playing with other puppy Pokémon, it sometimes gets bullied.
Fact: Although it looks frightening, it is actually kind and affectionate. It is very popular among women.

Name: Granbull
Bio: Although it’s popular with young people, Granbull is timid and sensitive, so it’s totally incompetent as a watchdog.
Fact: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If at­ tacked, it flails about to fend off its attacker.

Name: Qwilfish
Bio: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.
Fact: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

Name: Scizor
Bio: Evolved by way of a curious item. The shell covering its body has been shown to be stronger than forged steel.
Fact: It swings its eye- patterned pincers up to scare its foes. This makes it look like it has three heads.

Name: Shuckle
Bio: The berries stored in its vaselike shell eventually become a thick, pulpy juice.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (230) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Heracross
Bio: This Pokémon has an unparalleled horn. Heracross itself demonstrates tremendous power—it's capable of throwing several people trained in the traditional arts of war at once.
Fact: This powerful Pokémon thrusts its prized horn under its enemies’ bellies then lifts and throws them.

Name: Sneasel
Bio: Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.
Fact: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it sud­ denly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

Name: Teddiursa
Bio: It licks its paws because of the sweet honey that has soaked into them. It is cunning, stealing into the nests of Combee and taking for itself the honey that the Combee have amassed.
Fact: If it finds honey, its crescent mark glows. It always licks its paws because they are soaked with honey.

Name: Ursaring
Bio: When the cold season arrives in Hisui, this Pokémon will wander fields and mountains alike in search of its favorite berries. Ursaring's hunger during this time makes it a ferocious danger.
Fact: Although it is a good climber, it prefers to snap trees with its forelegs and eat fallen BERRIES.

Name: Slugma
Bio: Slugma does not have any blood in its body. Instead, intensely hot magma circulates throughout this Pokémon’s body, carrying essential nutrients and oxygen to its organs.
Fact: It never sleeps. It has to keep moving because if it stopped, its magma body would cool and harden.

Name: Magcargo
Bio: Magcargo’s body temperature is approximately 18,000 degrees Fahrenheit. Water is vaporized on contact. If this Pokémon is caught in the rain, the raindrops instantly turn into steam, cloaking the area in a thick fog.
Fact: The shell on its back is just skin that has cooled and hardened. It breaks easily with a slight touch.

Name: Swinub
Bio: Swinub excels at sniffing out mushrooms buried beneath grass or snow. Since ancient times, the people of Hisui have often relied upon this skill.
Fact: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

Name: Piloswine
Bio: The long fur of this Pokémon covers its eyes, ears, and even limbs, allowing Piloswine to resist harshly frigid conditions. The Pokémon's white tusks can be used to defeat its enemies.
Fact: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

Name: Corsola
Bio: These Pokémon live in warm seas. In prehistoric times, many lived in the oceans around the Galar region as well.
Fact: It continuously sheds and grows. The tip of its head is prized as a treasure for its beauty.

Name: Remoraid
Bio: Spits water from its mouth with incredible accuracy. It captures Burmy by shooting them down off the branches from which they dangle.
Fact: It has superb ac­ curacy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

Name: Octillery
Bio: While Octillery still shoots water from its mouth, the drastic anatomical difference between it and Remoraid meant that for a long time, no one believed the former evolved from the latter.
Fact: It traps enemies with its suction- cupped tentacles then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Delibird
Bio: It has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it’s always scrounging around for more food.
Fact: It carries food all day long. There are tales about lost people who were saved by the food it had.

Name: Mantine
Bio: This calm and gentle Pokémon swims gracefully through the sea. After building speed, it can leap out of the water. It is often misidentified as a bird Pokémon due to this behavior.
Fact: As it majestically swims, it doesn't care if REMORAID attach to it for scavenging its leftovers.

Name: Skarmory
Bio: People fashion swords from Skarmory’s shed feathers, so this Pokémon is a popular element in heraldic designs.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (140) of all 102 Flying-type Pokémon.

Name: Houndour
Bio: They make repeated eerie howls before dawn to call attention to their pack.
Fact: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

Name: Houndoom
Bio: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.
Fact: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.

Name: Kingdra
Bio: Scales shed by this Pokémon have such a splendorous gleam to them that they’ve been given to royalty as gifts.
Fact: It is said that it usually hides in underwater caves. It can create whirlpools by yawning.

Name: Phanpy
Bio: Phanpy uses its long nose to shower itself. When others gather around, they thoroughly douse each other with water. These Pokémon can be seen drying their soaking-wet bodies at the edge of water.
Fact: It swings its long snout around play­ fully, but because it is so strong, that can be dan­ gerous.

Name: Donphan
Bio: If Donphan were to tackle with its hard body, even a house could be destroyed. Using its massive strength, the Pokémon helps clear rock and mud slides that block mountain trails.
Fact: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its TACKLE is strong enough to knock down a house.

Name: Porygon2
Bio: A bizarre item caused this Pokémon to evolve. While it now exhibits many new gestures and expressions, its biology remains inscrutable.
Fact: This upgraded version of PORYGON is designed for space exploration. It can't fly, though.

Name: Stantler
Bio: Its strangely shaped antlers have the power to bewitch those who see them. Medicine made by grinding up the black orbs from fallen antlers is an effective treatment for insomnia.
Fact: The curved antlers subtly change the flow of air to create a strange space where real­ ity is distorted.

Name: Smeargle
Bio: It draws symbols with the fluid that oozes from the tip of its tail. Depending on the symbol, Smeargle fanatics will pay big money for them.
Fact: A special fluid oozes from the tip of its tail. It paints the fluid everywhere to mark its territory.

Name: Tyrogue
Bio: Even though it is small, it can’t be ignored because it will slug any handy target without warning.
Fact: It is always bursting with en­ ergy. To make it­ self stronger, it keeps on fighting even if it loses.

Name: Hitmontop
Bio: After doing a handstand to throw off the opponent’s timing, it presents its fancy kick moves.
Fact: If you become enchanted by its smooth, elegant, dance-like kicks, you may get drilled hard.

Name: Smoochum
Bio: This is a very curious Pokémon. Smoochum decides what it likes and dislikes by touching things with its lips.
Fact: Its lips are the most sensitive parts on its body. It always uses its lips first to examine things.

Name: Elekid
Bio: They generate electricity by spinning their arms. During a thunderstorm, if one hears the lively voices of children out in the wilderness, what one is actually hearing are the voices of Elekid.
Fact: It rotates its arms to generate electricity, but it tires easily, so it charges up only a little bit.

Name: Magby
Bio: This Pokémon lives in volcanic areas. With each breath, sparks spurt from its mouth and nose. When Magby is in good health, its flames gain a yellow tint.
Fact: Each and every time it inhales and exhales, hot embers dribble out of its mouth and nostrils.

Name: Miltank
Bio: This Pokémon needs to be milked every day, or else it will fall ill. The flavor of Miltank milk changes with the seasons.
Fact: Its milk is packed with nutrition, making it the ultimate beverage for the sick or weary.

Name: Blissey
Bio: A kindhearted Pokémon that will care for any sick person or Pokémon until their health improves. The eggs it lays are delicious and bring good fortune to those who eat them.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (255) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Raikou
Bio: Raikou embodies the speed of lightning. The roars of this Pokémon send shock waves shuddering through the air and shake the ground as if lightning bolts had come crashing down.
Fact: The rain clouds it carries let it fire thunderbolts at will. They say that it descended with lightning.

Name: Entei
Bio: Entei embodies the passion of magma. This Pokémon is thought to have been born in the eruption of a volcano. It sends up massive bursts of fire that utterly consume all that they touch.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (115) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Suicune
Bio: Suicune embodies the compassion of a pure spring of water. It runs across the land with gracefulness. This Pokémon has the power to purify dirty water.
Fact: Said to be the reincarnation of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

Name: Larvitar
Bio: It feeds on soil. After it has eaten a large mountain, it will fall asleep so it can grow.
Fact: It is born deep underground. It can't emerge until it has entirely consumed the soil around it.

Name: Pupitar
Bio: It will not stay still, even while it’s a pupa. It already has arms and legs under its solid shell.
Fact: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its THRASHING can topple a mountain.

Name: Tyranitar
Bio: The quakes caused when it walks make even great mountains crumble and change the surrounding terrain.
Fact: The only Rock/Dark type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Lugia
Bio: Lugia’s wings pack devastating power—a light fluttering of its wings can blow apart regular houses. As a result, this Pokémon chooses to live out of sight deep under the sea.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (154) of all 90 Psychic-type Pokémon.

Name: Ho-Oh
Bio: Ho-Oh’s feathers glow in seven colors depending on the angle at which they are struck by light. These feathers are said to bring happiness to the bearers. This Pokémon is said to live at the foot of a rainbow.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (154) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Celebi
Bio: This Pokémon traveled through time to come from the future. It bolsters grass and trees with its own strength, and it can heal wounds, too.
Fact: This Pokémon wan­ ders across time. Grass and trees flourish in the forests in which it has appeared.

Name: Treecko
Bio: Treecko is cool, calm, and collected—it never panics under any situation. If a bigger foe were to glare at this Pokémon, it would glare right back without conceding an inch of ground.
Fact: TREECKO has small hooks on the bottom of its feet that enable it to scale vertical walls. This Pokémon attacks by slamming foes with its thick tail.

Name: Grovyle
Bio: This Pokémon adeptly flies from branch to branch in trees. In a forest, no Pokémon can ever hope to catch a fleeing Grovyle however fast they may be.
Fact: The leaves growing out of GROVYLE’s body are convenient for camouflaging it from enemies in the forest. This Pokémon is a master at climbing trees in jungles.

Name: Sceptile
Bio: Sceptile has seeds growing on its back. They are said to be bursting with nutrients that revitalize trees. This Pokémon raises the trees in a forest with loving care.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (120) of all 106 Grass-type Pokémon.

Name: Torchic
Bio: Torchic has a place inside its body where it keeps its flame. Give it a hug—it will be glowing with warmth. This Pokémon is covered all over by a fluffy coat of down.
Fact: TORCHIC sticks with its TRAINER, following behind with unsteady steps. This Pokémon breathes fire of over 1,800 degrees F, including fireballs that leave the foe scorched black.

Name: Combusken
Bio: Combusken battles with the intensely hot flames it spews from its beak and with outstandingly destructive kicks. This Pokémon’s cry is very loud and distracting.
Fact: COMBUSKEN toughens up its legs and thighs by running through fields and mountains. This Pokémon’s legs possess both speed and power, enabling it to dole out ten kicks in one second.

Name: Blaziken
Bio: Blaziken has incredibly strong legs—it can easily clear a 30-story building in one leap. This Pokémon’s blazing punches leave its foes scorched and blackened.
Fact: In battle, BLAZIKEN blows out intense flames from its wrists and attacks foes courageously. The stronger the foe, the more intensely this Pokémon’s wrists burn.

Name: Mudkip
Bio: In water, Mudkip breathes using the gills on its cheeks. If it is faced with a tight situation in battle, this Pokémon will unleash its amazing power—it can crush rocks bigger than itself.
Fact: The fin on MUDKIP’s head acts as highly sensitive radar. Using this fin to sense movements of water and air, this Pokémon can determine what is taking place around it without using its eyes.

Name: Marshtomp
Bio: Marshtomp is much faster at traveling through mud than it is at swimming. This Pokémon’s hindquarters exhibit obvious development, giving it the ability to walk on just its hind legs.
Fact: The surface of MARSHTOMP’s body is enveloped by a thin, sticky film that enables it to live on land. This Pokémon plays in mud on beaches when the ocean tide is low.

Name: Swampert
Bio: Swampert predicts storms by sensing subtle differences in the sounds of waves and tidal winds with its fins. If a storm is approaching, it piles up boulders to protect itself.
Fact: SWAMPERT is very strong. It has enough power to easily drag a boulder weighing more than a ton. This Pokémon also has powerful vision that lets it see even in murky water.

Name: Poochyena
Bio: Poochyena is an omnivore—it will eat anything. A distinguishing feature is how large its fangs are compared to its body. This Pokémon tries to intimidate its foes by making the hair on its tail bristle out.
Fact: At first sight, POOCHYENA takes a bite at anything that moves. This Pokémon chases after prey until the victim becomes exhausted. However, it may turn tail if the prey strikes back.

Name: Mightyena
Bio: Mightyena travel and act as a pack in the wild. The memory of its life in the wild compels the Pokémon to obey only those Trainers that it recognizes to possess superior skill.
Fact: MIGHTYENA gives obvious signals when it is preparing to attack. It starts to growl deeply and then flattens its body. This Pokémon will bite savagely with its sharply pointed fangs.

Name: Zigzagoon
Bio: Zigzagoon that adapted to regions outside Galar acquired this appearance. If you’ve lost something, this Pokémon can likely find it.
Fact: ZIGZAGOON restlessly wanders everywhere at all times. This Pokémon does so because it is very curious. It becomes interested in anything that it happens to see.

Name: Linoone
Bio: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.
Fact: LINOONE always runs full speed and only in straight lines. If facing an obstacle, it makes a right-angle turn to evade it. This Pokémon is very challenged by gently curving roads.

Name: Wurmple
Bio: Likes sap and is abundant in the wild. Why it evolves into various different forms is unknown. One cannot tell from a Wurmple's appearance which form it will take when it evolves.
Fact: Using the spikes on its rear end, WURMPLE peels the bark off trees and feeds on the sap that oozes out. This Pokémon’s feet are tipped with suction pads that allow it to cling to glass without slipping.

Name: Silcoon
Bio: Wraps itself in thin strings of silk while it stores energy for evolution. It can't extend its limbs and its movement is slow, but its eyes keep a sharp lookout—Silcoon is always on guard.
Fact: SILCOON tethers itself to a tree branch using silk to keep from falling. There, this Pokémon hangs quietly while it awaits evolution. It peers out of the silk cocoon through a small hole.

Name: Beautifly
Bio: A colorful and incredibly beautiful but also greedy Pokémon. In an effort to keep its favorite food all to itself, it will chase away Combee as they try to gather nectar.
Fact: BEAUTIFLY’s favorite food is the sweet pollen of flowers. If you want to see this Pokémon, just leave a potted flower by an open window. BEAUTIFLY is sure to come looking for pollen.

Name: Cascoon
Bio: The silk coating its body is thin but sufficiently strong. Cascoon's silk has a luster and texture superior to that of Silcoon's, and clothes made using Cascoon silk are regarded as top-notch.
Fact: CASCOON makes its protective cocoon by wrapping its body entirely with a fine silk from its mouth. Once the silk goes around its body, it hardens. This Pokémon prepares for its evolution inside the cocoon.

Name: Dustox
Bio: Tends to be drawn to bonfires on dark nights. Difficult to chase away from settlements because of the way it scatters highly toxic scales.
Fact: DUSTOX is instinctively drawn to light. Swarms of this Pokémon are attracted by the bright lights of cities, where they wreak havoc by stripping the leaves off roadside trees for food.

Name: Lotad
Bio: Its leaf grew too large for it to live on land. That is how it began to live floating in the water.
Fact: LOTAD live in ponds and lakes, where they float on the surface. It grows weak if its broad leaf dies. On rare occasions, this Pokémon travels on land in search of clean water.

Name: Lombre
Bio: It lives at the water’s edge where it is sunny. It sleeps on a bed of water grass by day and becomes active at night.
Fact: LOMBRE is nocturnal - it will get active after dusk. It is also a mischief-maker. When this Pokémon spots anglers, it tugs on their fishing lines from beneath the surface and enjoys their consternation.

Name: Ludicolo
Bio: If it hears festive music, it begins moving in rhythm in order to amplify its power.
Fact: LUDICOLO begins dancing as soon as it hears cheerful, festive music. This Pokémon is said to appear when it hears the singing of children on hiking outings.

Name: Seedot
Bio: It attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. Strong winds can sometimes make it fall.
Fact: SEEDOT attaches itself to a tree branch using the top of its head. It sucks moisture from the tree while hanging off the branch. The more water it drinks, the glossier this Pokémon’s body becomes.

Name: Nuzleaf
Bio: They live in holes bored in large trees. The sound of Nuzleaf’s grass flute fills listeners with dread.
Fact: NUZLEAF live in densely overgrown forests. They occasionally venture out of the forest to startle people. This Pokémon dislikes having its long nose pinched.

Name: Shiftry
Bio: It lives quietly in the deep forest. It is said to create chilly winter winds with the fans it holds.
Fact: SHIFTRY is a mysterious Pokémon that is said to live atop towering trees dating back over a thousand years. It creates terrific windstorms with the fans it holds.

Name: Taillow
Bio: Taillow is young—it has only just left its nest. As a result, it sometimes becomes lonesome and cries at night. This Pokémon feeds on Wurmple that live in forests.
Fact: TAILLOW courageously stands its ground against foes, however strong they may be. This gutsy Pokémon will remain defiant even after a loss. On the other hand, it cries loudly if it becomes hungry.

Name: Swellow
Bio: Swellow is very conscientious about the upkeep of its glossy wings. Once two Swellow are gathered, they diligently take care of cleaning each other’s wings.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (125) of all 115 Normal-type Pokémon.

Name: Wingull
Bio: It soars on updrafts without flapping its wings. It makes a nest on sheer cliffs at the sea’s edge.
Fact: WINGULL has the habit of carrying prey and valuables in its beak and hiding them in all sorts of locations. This Pokémon rides the winds and flies as if it were skating across the sky.

Name: Pelipper
Bio: Skimming the water’s surface, it dips its large bill in the sea, scoops up food and water, and carries it.
Fact: PELIPPER is a flying transporter that carries small Pokémon and eggs inside its massive bill. This Pokémon builds its nest on steep cliffs facing the sea.

Name: Ralts
Bio: Tends to prefer people with a chipper disposition to those who are gloomy, but it has shown no discrimination with regard to age or gender. Needs more research.
Fact: RALTS senses the emotions of people using the horns on its head. This Pokémon rarely appears before people. But when it does, it draws closer if it senses that the person has a positive disposition.

Name: Kirlia
Bio: It resembles a maiden in appearance, but it wields strange powers to project visions of paradise. I suspect the crimson ornaments on its head are the key to its abilities.
Fact: It is said that a KIRLIA that is exposed to the positive emotions of its TRAINER grows beautiful. This Pokémon controls psychokinetic powers with its highly developed brain.

Name: Gardevoir
Bio: It will dedicate itself to defending a master it has come to adore. Its pure white dress, reminiscent of those worn by ladies of nobility, is the dress of one who is willing to risk their life.
Fact: GARDEVOIR has the ability to read the future. If it senses impending danger to its TRAINER, this Pokémon is said to unleash its psychokinetic energy at full power.

Name: Surskit
Bio: It lives in ponds and marshes that feature lots of plant life. It often fights with Dewpider, whose habitat and diet are similar.
Fact: From the tips of its feet, SURSKIT secretes an oil that enables it to walk on water as if it were skating. This Pokémon feeds on microscopic organisms in ponds and lakes.

Name: Masquerain
Bio: Its thin, winglike antennae are highly absorbent. It waits out rainy days in tree hollows.
Fact: MASQUERAIN intimidates enemies with the eyelike patterns on its antennas. This Pokémon flaps its four wings to freely fly in any direction - even sideways and backwards - as if it were a helicopter.

Name: Shroomish
Bio: If Shroomish senses danger, it shakes its body and scatters spores from the top of its head. This Pokémon’s spores are so toxic, they make trees and weeds wilt.
Fact: SHROOMISH live in damp soil in the dark depths of forests. They are often found keeping still under fallen leaves. This Pokémon feeds on compost that is made up of fallen, rotted leaves.

Name: Breloom
Bio: The seeds ringing Breloom’s tail are made of hardened toxic spores. It is horrible to eat the seeds. Just taking a bite of this Pokémon’s seed will cause your stomach to rumble.
Fact: BRELOOM closes in on its foe with light and sprightly footwork, then throws punches with its stretchy arms. This Pokémon’s fighting technique puts boxers to shame.

Name: Slakoth
Bio: Slakoth’s heart beats just once a minute. Whatever happens, it is content to loaf around motionless. It is rare to see this Pokémon in motion.
Fact: SLAKOTH lolls around for over twenty hours every day. Because it moves so little, it does not need much food. This Pokémon’s sole daily meal consists of just three leaves.

Name: Vigoroth
Bio: Vigoroth is simply incapable of remaining still. Even when it tries to sleep, the blood in its veins grows agitated, compelling this Pokémon to run wild throughout the jungle before it can settle down.
Fact: VIGOROTH is always itching and agitated to go on a wild rampage. It simply can’t tolerate sitting still for even a minute. This Pokémon’s stress level rises if it can’t be moving constantly.

Name: Slaking
Bio: Wherever Slaking live, rings of over a yard in diameter appear in grassy fields. They are made by the Pokémon as it eats all the grass within reach while lying prone on the ground.
Fact: SLAKING spends all day lying down and lolling about. It eats grass growing within its reach. If it eats all the grass it can reach, this Pokémon reluctantly moves to another spot.

Name: Nincada
Bio: It can sometimes live underground for more than 10 years. It absorbs nutrients from the roots of trees.
Fact: The only Bug/Ground type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Ninjask
Bio: This Pokémon is so quick, it is said to be able to avoid any attack. It loves to feed on tree sap.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (160) of all 84 Bug-type Pokémon.

Name: Shedinja
Bio: A strange Pokémon—it flies without moving its wings, has a hollow shell for a body, and does not breathe.
Fact: The only Bug/Ghost type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Whismur
Bio: When Whismur cries, the sound of its own voice startles it, making the Pokémon cry even louder. It cries until it’s exhausted, then it falls asleep.
Fact: Normally, WHISMUR’s voice is very quiet - it is barely audible even if one is paying close attention. However, if this Pokémon senses danger, it starts crying at an earsplitting volume.

Name: Loudred
Bio: The force of this Pokémon’s loud voice isn’t just the sound—it’s also the wave of air pressure that blows opponents away and damages them.
Fact: LOUDRED’s bellowing can completely decimate a wood-frame house. It uses its voice to punish its foes. This Pokémon’s round ears serve as loudspeakers.

Name: Exploud
Bio: This Pokémon can do more than just shout. To communicate with others of its kind, it’ll emit all sorts of sounds from the holes in its body.
Fact: EXPLOUD triggers earthquakes with the tremors it creates by bellowing. If this Pokémon violently inhales from the ports on its body, it’s a sign that it is preparing to let loose a huge bellow.

Name: Makuhita
Bio: There’s a rumor of a traditional recipe for stew that Trainers can use to raise strong Makuhita.
Fact: MAKUHITA is tenacious - it will keep getting up and attacking its foe however many times it is knocked down. Every time it gets back up, this Pokémon stores more energy in its body for evolving.

Name: Hariyama
Bio: Hariyama that are big and fat aren’t necessarily strong. There are some small ones that move nimbly and use moves skillfully.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (144) of all 61 Fighting-type Pokémon.

Name: Azurill
Bio: Although Azurill are normally docile, an angry one will swing around the big ball on its tail and try to smash its opponents.
