Fact: AZURILL spins its tail as if it were a lasso, then hurls it far. The momentum of the throw sends its body flying, too. Using this unique action, one of these Pokémon managed to hurl itself a record 33 feet.

Name: Nosepass
Bio: Once the people of Hisui discovered that its red nose always points north, they grew to rely on it greatly when traveling afar. The nose seems to work in a similar way to ancient compasses.
Fact: NOSEPASS’s magnetic nose is always pointed to the north. If two of these Pokémon meet, they cannot turn their faces to each other when they are close because their magnetic noses repel one another.

Name: Skitty
Bio: Skitty is known to chase around playfully after its own tail. In the wild, this Pokémon lives in holes in the trees of forests. It is very popular as a pet because of its adorable looks.
Fact: SKITTY has the habit of becoming fascinated by moving objects and chasing them around. This Pokémon is known to chase after its own tail and become dizzy.

Name: Delcatty
Bio: Delcatty sleeps anywhere it wants without keeping a permanent nest. If other Pokémon approach it as it sleeps, this Pokémon will never fight—it will just move away somewhere else.
Fact: DELCATTY prefers to live an unfettered existence in which it can do as it pleases at its own pace. Because this Pokémon eats and sleeps whenever it decides, its daily routines are completely random.

Name: Sableye
Bio: It feeds on gemstone crystals. In darkness, its eyes sparkle with the glitter of jewels.
Fact: SABLEYE lead quiet lives deep inside caverns. They are feared, however, because these Pokémon are thought to steal the spirits of people when their eyes burn with a sinister glow in the darkness.

Name: Mawile
Bio: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.
Fact: MAWHILE’s huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed. Its docile-looking face serves to lull its foe into letting down its guard. When the foe least expects it, MAWHILE chomps it with its gaping jaws.

Name: Aron
Bio: When Aron evolves, its steel armor peels off. In ancient times, people would collect Aron’s shed armor and make good use of it in their daily lives.
Fact: This Pokémon has a body of steel. To make its body, ARON feeds on iron ore that it digs from mountains. Occasionally, it causes major trouble by eating bridges and rails.

Name: Lairon
Bio: During territorial disputes, Lairon fight by slamming into each other. Close inspection of their steel armor reveals scratches and dents.
Fact: LAIRON tempers its steel body by drinking highly nutritious mineral springwater until it is bloated. This Pokémon makes its nest close to springs of delicious water.

Name: Aggron
Bio: Long ago, there was a king who wore a helmet meant to resemble the head of an Aggron. He was trying to channel the Pokémon’s strength.
Fact: AGGRON claims an entire mountain as its own territory. It mercilessly beats up anything that violates its environment. This Pokémon vigilantly patrols its territory at all times.

Name: Meditite
Bio: Meditite heightens its inner energy through meditation. It survives on just one berry a day. Minimal eating is another aspect of this Pokémon’s training.
Fact: MEDITITE undertakes rigorous mental training deep in the mountains. However, whenever it meditates, this Pokémon always loses its concentration and focus. As a result, its training never ends.

Name: Medicham
Bio: Through the power of meditation, Medicham developed its sixth sense. It gained the ability to use psychokinetic powers. This Pokémon is known to meditate for a whole month without eating.
Fact: It is said that through meditation, MEDICHAM heightens energy inside its body and sharpens its sixth sense. This Pokémon hides its presence by merging itself with fields and mountains.

Name: Electrike
Bio: It stores electricity in its fur. It gives off sparks from all over its body in seasons when the air is dry.
Fact: ELECTRIKE stores electricity in its long body hair. This Pokémon stimulates its leg muscles with electric charges. These jolts of power give its legs explosive acceleration performance.

Name: Manectric
Bio: It rarely appears before people. It is said to nest where lightning has fallen.
Fact: MANECTRIC is constantly discharging electricity from its mane. The sparks sometimes ignite forest fires. When it enters a battle, this Pokémon creates thunderclouds.

Name: Plusle
Bio: When Plusle is cheering on its partner, it flashes with electric sparks from all over its body. If its partner loses, this Pokémon cries loudly.
Fact: PLUSLE always acts as a cheerleader for its partners. Whenever a teammate puts out a good effort in battle, this Pokémon shorts out its body to create the crackling noises of sparks to show its joy.

Name: Minun
Bio: Minun loves to cheer on its partner in battle. It gives off sparks from its body while it is doing so. If its partner is in trouble, this Pokémon gives off increasing amounts of sparks.
Fact: MINUN is more concerned about cheering on its partners than its own safety. It shorts out the electricity in its body to create brilliant showers of sparks to cheer on its teammates.

Name: Volbeat
Bio: Volbeat’s tail glows like a lightbulb. With other Volbeat, it uses its tail to draw geometric shapes in the night sky. This Pokémon loves the sweet aroma given off by Illumise.
Fact: With the arrival of night, VOLBEAT emits light from its tail. It communicates with others by adjusting the intensity and flashing of its light. This Pokémon is attracted by the sweet aroma of ILLUMISE.

Name: Illumise
Bio: Illumise leads a flight of illuminated Volbeat to draw signs in the night sky. This Pokémon is said to earn greater respect from its peers by composing more complex designs in the sky.
Fact: ILLUMISE attracts a swarm of VOLBEAT using a sweet fragrance. Once the VOLBEAT have gathered, this Pokémon leads the lit-up swarm in drawing geometric designs on the canvas of the night sky.

Name: Roselia
Bio: Though beautiful, it has highly poisonous thorns. There is an old tradition in my homeland wherein one would send these thorns to an opponent to challenge them to a duel.
Fact: ROSELIA shoots sharp thorns as projectiles at any opponent that tries to steal the flowers on its arms. The aroma of this Pokémon brings serenity to living things.

Name: Gulpin
Bio: Most of Gulpin’s body is made up of its stomach—its heart and brain are very small in comparison. This Pokémon’s stomach contains special enzymes that dissolve anything.
Fact: Virtually all of GULPIN’s body is its stomach. As a result, it can swallow something its own size. This Pokémon’s stomach contains a special fluid that digests anything.

Name: Swalot
Bio: Swalot has no teeth, so what it eats, it swallows whole, no matter what. Its cavernous mouth yawns widely. An automobile tire could easily fit inside this Pokémon’s mouth.
Fact: When SWALOT spots prey, it spurts out a hideously toxic fluid from its pores and sprays the target. Once the prey has weakened, this Pokémon gulps it down whole with its cavernous mouth.

Name: Carvanha
Bio: These Pokémon have sharp fangs and powerful jaws. Sailors avoid Carvanha dens at all costs.
Fact: CARVANHA’s strongly developed jaws and its sharply pointed fangs pack the destructive power to rip out boat hulls. Many boats have been attacked and sunk by this Pokémon.

Name: Sharpedo
Bio: This Pokémon is known as the Bully of the Sea. Any ship entering the waters Sharpedo calls home will be attacked—no exceptions.
Fact: Nicknamed “the bully of the sea,” SHARPEDO is widely feared. Its cruel fangs grow back immediately if they snap off. Just one of these Pokémon can thoroughly tear apart a supertanker.

Name: Wailmer
Bio: When it sucks in a large volume of seawater, it becomes like a big, bouncy ball. It eats a ton of food daily.
Fact: WAILMER’s nostrils are located above its eyes. This playful Pokémon loves to startle people by forcefully snorting out seawater it stores inside its body out of its nostrils.

Name: Wailord
Bio: Its immense size is the reason for its popularity. Wailord watching is a favorite sightseeing activity in various parts of the world.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (170) of all 141 Water-type Pokémon.

Name: Numel
Bio: Numel stores magma of almost 2,200 degrees Fahrenheit within its body. If it gets wet, the magma cools and hardens. In that event, the Pokémon’s body grows heavy and its movements become sluggish.
Fact: NUMEL is extremely dull witted - it doesn’t notice being hit. However, it can’t stand hunger for even a second. This Pokémon’s body is a seething cauldron of boiling magma.

Name: Camerupt
Bio: The humps on Camerupt’s back are formed by a transformation of its bones. They sometimes blast out molten magma. This Pokémon apparently erupts often when it is enraged.
Fact: CAMERUPT has a volcano inside its body. Magma of 18,000 degrees F courses through its body. Occasionally, the humps on this Pokémon’s back erupt, spewing the superheated magma.

Name: Torkoal
Bio: You find abandoned coal mines full of them. They dig tirelessly in search of coal.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (140) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Spoink
Bio: Spoink keeps a pearl on top of its head. The pearl functions to amplify this Pokémon’s psychokinetic powers. It is therefore on a constant search for a bigger pearl.
Fact: SPOINK bounces around on its tail. The shock of its bouncing makes its heart pump. As a result, this Pokémon cannot afford to stop bouncing - if it stops, its heart will stop.

Name: Grumpig
Bio: Grumpig uses the black pearls on its body to wield its fantastic powers. When it is doing so, it dances bizarrely. This Pokémon’s black pearls are valuable as works of art.
Fact: GRUMPIG uses the black pearls on its body to amplify its psychic power waves for gaining total control over its foe. When this Pokémon uses its special power, its snorting breath grows labored.

Name: Spinda
Bio: Each Spinda’s spot pattern is different. With its stumbling movements, it evades opponents’ attacks brilliantly!
Fact: All the SPINDA that exist in the world are said to have utterly unique spot patterns. The shaky, tottering steps of this Pokémon give it the appearance of dancing.

Name: Trapinch
Bio: It makes an inescapable conical pit and lies in wait at the bottom for prey to come tumbling down.
Fact: TRAPINCH’s nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit dug in sand. This Pokémon patiently waits for prey to tumble down the pit. Its giant jaws have enough strength to crush even boulders.

Name: Vibrava
Bio: To help make its wings grow, it dissolves quantities of prey in its digestive juices and guzzles them down every day.
Fact: To make prey faint, VIBRAVA generates ultrasonic waves by vigorously making its two wings vibrate. This Pokémon’s ultrasonic waves are so powerful, they can bring on headaches in people.

Name: Flygon
Bio: It is nicknamed the Desert Spirit because the flapping of its wings sounds like a woman singing.
Fact: FLYGON is nicknamed “the elemental spirit of the desert.” Because its flapping wings whip up a cloud of sand, this Pokémon is always enveloped in a sandstorm while flying.

Name: Cacnea
Bio: The more arid and harsh the environment, the more pretty and fragrant a flower Cacnea grows. This Pokémon battles by wildly swinging its thorny arms.
Fact: CACNEA lives in arid locations such as deserts. It releases a strong aroma from its flower to attract prey. When prey comes near, this Pokémon shoots sharp thorns from its body to bring the victim down.

Name: Cacturne
Bio: If a traveler is going through a desert in the thick of night, Cacturne will follow in a ragtag group. The Pokémon are biding their time, waiting for the traveler to tire and become incapable of moving.
Fact: During the daytime, CACTURNE remains unmoving so that it does not lose any moisture to the harsh desert sun. This Pokémon becomes active at night when the temperature drops.

Name: Swablu
Bio: Since Swablu looks like a cumulus cloud, foes can have a hard time finding it. Apparently its wings turned white over many generations.
Fact: SWABLU has light and fluffy wings that are like cottony clouds. This Pokémon is not frightened of people. It lands on the heads of people and sits there like a cotton-fluff hat.

Name: Altaria
Bio: This Pokémon has a kind disposition, but if it’s provoked, it will threaten opponents with shrill cries before attacking them without mercy.
Fact: ALTARIA dances and wheels through the sky among billowing, cotton-like clouds. By singing melodies in its crystal-clear voice, this Pokémon makes its listeners experience dreamy wonderment.

Name: Zangoose
Bio: Zangoose usually stays on all fours, but when angered, it gets up on its hind legs and extends its claws. This Pokémon shares a bitter rivalry with Seviper that dates back over generations.
Fact: Memories of battling its arch-rival SEVIPER are etched into every cell of ZANGOOSE’s body. This Pokémon adroitly dodges attacks with incredible agility.

Name: Seviper
Bio: Seviper’s swordlike tail serves two purposes—it slashes foes and douses them with secreted poison. This Pokémon will not give up its long-running blood feud with Zangoose.
Fact: SEVIPER shares a generations-long feud with ZANGOOSE. The scars on its body are evidence of vicious battles. This Pokémon attacks using its sword- edged tail.

Name: Lunatone
Bio: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.
Fact: LUNATONE was discovered at a location where a meteorite fell. As a result, some people theorize that this Pokémon came from space. However, no one has been able to prove this theory so far.

Name: Solrock
Bio: Solar energy is the source of its power, so it is strong during the daytime. When it spins, its body shines.
Fact: SOLROCK is a new species of Pokémon that is said to have fallen from space. It floats in air and moves silently. In battle, this Pokémon releases intensely bright light.

Name: Barboach
Bio: Makes its home in swamps with murky water. The poor visibility hides this Pokémon from predators, and the slime on its body makes grasping it difficult.
Fact: BARBOACH’s sensitive whiskers serve as a superb radar system. This Pokémon hides in mud, leaving only its two whiskers exposed while it waits for prey to come along.

Name: Whiscash
Bio: Strikes its caudal fin against the swamp bed to shake the ground and startle its prey. It will then swallow the fleeing prey whole. People mistook this behavior as the cause of earthquakes.
Fact: WHISCASH is extremely territorial. Just one of these Pokémon will claim a large pond as its exclusive territory. If a foe approaches it, it thrashes about and triggers a massive earthquake.

Name: Corphish
Bio: It was originally a Pokémon from afar that escaped to the wild. It can adapt to the dirtiest river.
Fact: CORPHISH were originally foreign Pokémon that were imported as pets. They eventually turned up in the wild. This Pokémon is very hardy and has greatly increased its population.

Name: Crawdaunt
Bio: A brutish Pokémon that loves to battle. It will crash itself into any foe that approaches its nest.
Fact: CRAWDAUNT has an extremely violent nature that compels it to challenge other living things to battle. Other life-forms refuse to live in ponds inhabited by this Pokémon, making them desolate places.

Name: Baltoy
Bio: It was discovered in ancient ruins. While moving, it constantly spins. It stands on one foot even when asleep.
Fact: BALTOY moves while spinning around on its one foot. Primitive wall paintings depicting this Pokémon living among people were discovered in some ancient ruins.

Name: Claydol
Bio: It appears to have been born from clay dolls made by ancient people. It uses telekinesis to float and move.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (120) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Lileep
Bio: Lileep clings to rocks on the seabed. When prey comes close, this Pokémon entangles it with petallike tentacles.
Fact: LILEEP became extinct approximately a hundred million years ago. This ancient Pokémon attaches itself to a rock on the seafloor and catches approaching prey using tentacles shaped like flower petals.

Name: Cradily
Bio: Once Cradily catches prey in its tentacles, it digests them whole and absorbs their nutrients.
Fact: CRADILY roams around the ocean floor in search of food. This Pokémon freely extends its tree trunk-like neck and captures unwary prey using its eight tentacles.

Name: Anorith
Bio: Anorith can swim swiftly by pulling its eight wings through the water like oars on a boat. This Pokémon is an ancestor of modern bug Pokémon.
Fact: ANORITH was regenerated from a prehistoric fossil. This primitive Pokémon once lived in warm seas. It grips its prey firmly between its two large claws.

Name: Armaldo
Bio: Though it lives on land, it’s also a good swimmer. It dives into the ocean in search of prey, using its sharp claws to take down its quarry.
Fact: ARMALDO’s tough armor makes all attacks bounce off. This Pokémon’s two enormous claws can be freely extended or contracted. They have the power to punch right through a steel slab.

Name: Feebas
Bio: It is a shabby and ugly Pokémon. However, it is very hardy and can survive on little water.
Fact: FEEBAS’s fins are ragged and tattered from the start of its life. Because of its shoddy appearance, this Pokémon is largely ignored. It is capable of living in both the sea and in rivers.

Name: Milotic
Bio: It’s said that a glimpse of a Milotic and its beauty will calm any hostile emotions you’re feeling.
Fact: MILOTIC is said to be the most beautiful of all the Pokémon. It has the power to becalm such emotions as anger and hostility to quell bitter feuding.

Name: Castform
Bio: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!
Fact: CASTFORM’s appearance changes with the weather. This Pokémon gained the ability to use the vast power of nature to protect its tiny body.

Name: Kecleon
Bio: Its color changes for concealment and also when its mood or health changes. The darker the color, the healthier it is.
Fact: KECLEON is capable of changing its body colors at will to blend in with its surroundings. There is one exception - this Pokémon can’t change the zigzag pattern on its belly.

Name: Shuppet
Bio: There’s a proverb that says, “Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk.”
Fact: SHUPPET is attracted by feelings of jealousy and vindictiveness. If someone develops strong feelings of vengeance, this Pokémon will appear in a swarm and line up beneath the eaves of that person’s home.

Name: Banette
Bio: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.
Fact: BANETTE generates energy for laying strong curses by sticking pins into its own body. This Pokémon was originally a pitiful plush doll that was thrown away.

Name: Duskull
Bio: I've heard that the children of Hisui all begin to behave once they've been told the story of how this Pokémon roams about before the witching hour to spirit away misbehaving children.
Fact: DUSKULL can pass through any wall no matter how thick it may be. Once this Pokémon chooses a target, it will doggedly pursue the intended victim until the break of dawn.

Name: Dusclops
Bio: There are rumors that peeking inside its bandage-wrapped body will cause one to get pulled in through the gaps between the bandages, never to return. I've been too scared to verify.
Fact: DUSCLOPS’s body is completely hollow - there is nothing at all inside. It is said that its body is like a black hole. This Pokémon will absorb anything into its body, but nothing will ever come back out.

Name: Tropius
Bio: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.
Fact: The bunches of fruit around TROPIUS’s neck are very popular with children. This Pokémon loves fruit, and eats it continuously. Apparently, its love for fruit resulted in its own outgrowth of fruit.

Name: Chimecho
Bio: Can emit waves of air powerful enough to knock out prey taller than itself. I hypothesize that it amplifies the faint sound of wind within its body.
Fact: CHIMECHO makes its cries echo inside its hollow body. When this Pokémon becomes enraged, its cries result in ultrasonic waves that have the power to knock foes flying.

Name: Absol
Bio: Because of this Pokémon’s ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.
Fact: Every time ABSOL appears before people, it is followed by a disaster such as an earthquake or a tidal wave. As a result, it came to be known as the disaster Pokémon.

Name: Wynaut
Bio: It tends to move in a pack with others. They cluster in a tight group to sleep in a cave.
Fact: WYNAUT can always be seen with a big, happy smile on its face. Look at its tail to determine if it is angry. When angered, this Pokémon will be slapping the ground with its tail.

Name: Snorunt
Bio: Arrives alongside the first snow. It's thought that homes Snorunt visit will prosper for many generations. By tradition, one might offer a lump of ice made from pure water at one's front door.
Fact: SNORUNT live in regions with heavy snowfall. In seasons without snow, such as spring and summer, this Pokémon steals away to live quietly among stalactites and stalagmites deep in caverns.

Name: Glalie
Bio: It covers its body with an armor of ice harder than steel. Uses its breath to freeze prey, which it then devours as if they were frozen desserts.
Fact: GLALIE has a body made of rock, which it hardens with an armor of ice. This Pokémon has the ability to freeze moisture in the atmosphere into any shape it desires.

Name: Spheal
Bio: During the season when drift ice approaches the shore, Spheal prefers living on the ice—where fewer predators lurk—rather than the land. Its fur retains heat superbly and resists harsh cold.
Fact: SPHEAL is much faster rolling than walking to get around. When groups of this Pokémon eat, they all clap at once to show their pleasure. Because of this, their mealtimes are noisy.

Name: Sealeo
Bio: Its white whiskers are very sensitive. Sealeo will balance Spheal on the tip of its nose, checking its scent and its feel to be sure the Spheal is healthy.
Fact: SEALEO has the habit of always juggling on the tip of its nose anything it sees for the first time. This Pokémon occasionally entertains itself by balancing and rolling a SPHEAL on its nose.

Name: Walrein
Bio: Its thick tusks are strong enough to shatter drift ice. They have been known to break, but they will grow back by the next year. The Hisui region is well known for these broken tusks.
Fact: WALREIN’s two massively developed tusks can totally shatter blocks of ice weighing ten tons with one blow. This Pokémon’s thick coat of blubber insulates it from subzero temperatures.

Name: Clamperl
Bio: Clamperl’s pearls are exceedingly precious. They can be more than 10 times as costly as Shellder’s pearls.
Fact: CLAMPERL’s sturdy shell is not only good for protection - it is also used for clamping and catching prey. A fully grown CLAMPERL’s shell will be scored with nicks and scratches all over.

Name: Huntail
Bio: Deep seas are their habitat. According to tradition, when Huntail wash up onshore, something unfortunate will happen.
Fact: HUNTAIL’s presence went unnoticed by people for a long time because it lives at extreme depths in the sea. This Pokémon’s eyes can see clearly even in the murky dark depths of the ocean.

Name: Gorebyss
Bio: It sucks bodily fluids out of its prey. The leftover meat sinks to the seafloor, where it becomes food for other Pokémon.
Fact: GOREBYSS lives in the southern seas at extreme depths. Its body is built to withstand the enormous pressure of water at incredible depths. Because of this, this Pokémon’s body is unharmed by ordinary attacks.

Name: Relicanth
Bio: This Pokémon was discovered during deep-sea exploration. Its appearance hasn’t changed in 100,000,000 years, so it’s called a living fossil.
Fact: RELICANTH is a Pokémon species that existed for a hundred million years without ever changing its form. This ancient Pokémon feeds on microscopic organisms with its toothless mouth.

Name: Luvdisc
Bio: Luvdisc makes its home in coral reefs in warm seas. It especially likes sleeping in the space between Corsola’s branches.
Fact: LUVDISC live in shallow seas in the tropics. This heart-shaped Pokémon earned its name by swimming after loving couples it spotted in the ocean’s waves.

Name: Bagon
Bio: Bagon is a solitary Pokémon that doesn’t form groups with others of its kind. It also has a head hard enough to cleave a boulder in one strike.
Fact: BAGON has a dream of one day soaring in the sky. In doomed efforts to fly, this Pokémon hurls itself off cliffs. As a result of its dives, its head has grown tough and as hard as tempered steel.

Name: Shelgon
Bio: Shelgon ignores its hunger entirely, never eating any food. Apparently, Shelgon will evolve once all its energy stores are used up.
Fact: Inside SHELGON’s armor-like shell, cells are in the midst of transformation to create an entirely new body. This Pokémon’s shell is extremely heavy, making its movements sluggish.

Name: Salamence
Bio: While basking in the joy of flight generally keeps this Pokémon in high spirits, Salamence turns into an uncontrollable menace if something angers it.
Fact: SALAMENCE came about as a result of a strong, long-held dream of growing wings. It is said that this powerful desire triggered a sudden mutation in this Pokémon’s cells, causing it to sprout its magnificent wings.

Name: Beldum
Bio: The cells in this Pokémon’s body are composed of magnetic material. Instead of blood, magnetic forces flow through Beldum’s body.
Fact: Instead of blood, a powerful magnetic force courses throughout BELDUM’s body. This Pokémon communicates with others by sending controlled pulses of magnetism.

Name: Metang
Bio: Using magnetic forces to stay aloft, this Pokémon flies at high speeds, weaving through harsh mountain terrain in pursuit of prey.
Fact: When two BELDUM fuse together, METANG is formed. The brains of the BELDUM are joined by a magnetic nervous system. By linking its brains magnetically, this Pokémon generates strong psychokinetic power.

Name: Metagross
Bio: Metagross is the result of the fusion of two Metang. This Pokémon defeats its opponents through use of its supercomputer-level brain.
Fact: METAGROSS has four brains in total. Combined, the four brains can breeze through difficult calculations faster than a supercomputer. This Pokémon can float in the air by tucking in its four legs.

Name: Regirock
Bio: Cutting-edge technology was used to study the internals of this Pokémon’s rock body, but nothing was found—not even a brain or a heart.
Fact: REGIROCK was sealed away by people long ago. If this Pokémon’s body is damaged in battle, it is said to seek out suitable rocks on its own to repair itself.

Name: Regice
Bio: This Pokémon’s body is made of solid ice. It’s said that Regice was born beneath thick ice in the ice age.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (200) of all 41 Ice-type Pokémon.

Name: Registeel
Bio: It’s rumored that this Pokémon was born deep underground in the planet’s mantle and that it emerged onto the surface 10,000 years ago.
Fact: REGISTEEL has a body that is harder than any kind of metal. Its body is apparently hollow. No one has any idea what this Pokémon eats.

Name: Latias
Bio: Latias is highly intelligent and capable of understanding human speech. It is covered with a glass-like down. The Pokémon enfolds its body with its down and refracts light to alter its appearance.
Fact: LATIAS is highly sensitive to the emotions of people. If it senses any hostility, this Pokémon ruffles the feathers all over its body and cries shrilly to intimidate the foe.

Name: Latios
Bio: Latios will only open its heart to a Trainer with a compassionate spirit. This Pokémon can fly faster than a jet plane by folding its forelegs to minimize air resistance.
Fact: LATIOS has the ability to make its foe see an image of what it has seen or imagines in its head. This Pokémon is intelligent and understands human speech.

Name: Kyogre
Bio: Kyogre is said to be the personification of the sea itself. Legends tell of its many clashes against Groudon, as each sought to gain the power of nature.
Fact: KYOGRE has the power to create massive rain clouds that cover the entire sky and bring about torrential downpours. This Pokémon saved people who were suffering from droughts.

Name: Groudon
Bio: Through Primal Reversion and with nature’s full power, it will take back its true form. It can cause magma to erupt and expand the landmass of the world.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (150) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Rayquaza
Bio: It flies forever through the ozone layer, consuming meteoroids for sustenance. The many meteoroids in its body provide the energy it needs to Mega Evolve.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (150) of all 102 Flying-type Pokémon.

Name: Jirachi
Bio: It’s believed that when this Pokémon wakes from its 1,000-year slumber, it will grant any wishes written on the notes attached to its head.
Fact: A legend states that JIRACHI will make true any wish that is written on notes attached to its head when it awakens. If this Pokémon senses danger, it will fight without awakening.

Name: Deoxys
Bio: Deoxys emerged from a virus that came from space. It is highly intelligent and wields psychokinetic powers. This Pokémon shoots lasers from the crystalline organ on its chest.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (150) of all 90 Psychic-type Pokémon.

Name: Turtwig
Bio: This Pokémon becomes more energetic the more sunlight there is. The part resembling a shell is similar to silt and is slightly damp and warm to the touch.
Fact: Made from soil, the shell on its back hardens when it drinks water. It lives along lakes.

Name: Grotle
Bio: Appears where there is clean spring water. The fruit that grows on the shrubs on its shell is sweet, nutritious, and truly delicious.
Fact: It lives along water in forests. In the daytime, it leaves the forest to sunbathe its treed shell.

Name: Torterra
Bio: This remarkable, large-bodied Pokémon would serve beautifully as borrowed scenery for a garden, and its strength is peerless. Torterra roams the wilderness in search of clean water.
Fact: The only Grass/Ground type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Chimchar
Bio: Full of vigor and always in high spirits. It was once known by the name ”Lantern-Tail” and feared as some kind of apparition.
Fact: It agilely scales sheer cliffs to live atop craggy mountains. Its fire is put out when it sleeps.

Name: Monferno
Bio: The deeper the blue on its face, the more powerful it will grow to become. It leaps about every which way and lands powerful blows against its opponents with the flame on its tail.
Fact: To intimidate attackers, it stretches the fire on its tail to make itself appear bigger.

Name: Infernape
Bio: A tall, hardy Pokémon with a dazzling appearance. It shrouds itself in flame and battles as if engaged in dance—truly a sight to behold.
Fact: It uses a special kind of martial arts involving all its limbs. Its fire never goes out.

Name: Piplup
Bio: Prefers cold climes and appears along coasts. It's an adorable little thing—as cute as any child—but it's also prideful, unwilling to accept handouts from people.
Fact: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Prinplup
Bio: It swims gracefully through the frigid sea and sings with a voice like the roaring tide. It has powerful, sturdy wings and dignity to match.
Fact: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

Name: Empoleon
Bio: Since ancient times, it has been revered by the people of Hisui, who call it the Master of the Waves. Its wings are a match for even master-crafted blades.
Fact: The only Water/Steel type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Starly
Bio: They live in the fields and mountains, gathering in large flocks. Their cries are quite obnoxious. Though small, their wings are strong—a strike from them leaves pain that persists for a week.
Fact: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

Name: Staravia
Bio: They form remarkably large flocks and are constantly fighting amongst themselves. I suspect that those with magnificent plumes on their heads are the strong ones.
Fact: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

Name: Staraptor
Bio: It has left the flock, having gained strength enough to survive on its own. The astounding force with which Staraptor flies through the air allows it to carry away large, burly targets.
Fact: It has a savage nature. It will courageously challenge foes that are much larger.

Name: Bidoof
Bio: Bidoof has an unsophisticated face and is rarely flustered by anything. There have been incidents involving Bidoof sauntering into villages and gnawing on the houses without a single care.
Fact: With nerves of steel, nothing can perturb it. It is more agile and active than it appears.

Name: Bibarel
Bio: Bibarel fur repels water and is also a fantastic material for heat retention. These Pokémon create dams on rivers to live in.
Fact: The only Normal/Water type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Kricketot
Bio: When the trees take on new hues, more of these Pokémon appear. The tone they create by striking their antennae together resembles that of the marimba, an instrument of foreign lands.
Fact: It shakes its head back to front, causing its antennae to hit each other and sound like a xylophone.

Name: Kricketune
Bio: It uses its cutlass-like arms to produce sound, the melody of which varies from individual to individual. It is a worthwhile endeavor to seek out one's favorite tunes.
Fact: It crosses its knifelike arms in front of its chest when it cries. It can compose melodies ad lib.

Name: Shinx
Bio: Shakes its body to generate electricity. Its stature belies its aggression—one must be patient to tame this Pokémon.
Fact: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

Name: Luxio
Bio: Proudly uses its electrified claws as weapons. It seems to be a gracious Pokémon, evenly sharing the spoils of the hunt with others of its kind.
Fact: Its claws loose electricity with enough amperage to cause fainting. They live in small groups.

Name: Luxray
FirstLocation: Soothing Wetlands (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: They form packs, each having one male as leader. Legends say that when Luxray's two eyes shimmer with gold, the Pokémon can see through anything.
Fact: It has eyes that can see through anything. It spots and captures prey hiding behind objects.

Name: Budew
Bio: When the sun's light strengthens, the bud atop this Pokémon's head opens. This is a sign to the people that the harsh winter is over, and the season of budding has begun.
Fact: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.

Name: Roserade
Bio: Hidden within the bouquet on each hand are thorned whips loaded with virulent poison. Roserade moves gracefully as it corners its prey and mercilessly lashes them with its whips.
Fact: It attracts prey with a sweet aroma, then downs it with thorny whips hidden in its arms.

Name: Cranidos
Bio: An incredibly rare sight. They duel each other by ramming their heads together, and the resulting sound echoes throughout the area like the pealing of a bell.
Fact: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

Name: Rampardos
Bio: Very little is known about its biology. Can knock down massive trees by smashing its beautiful, pearl-like crown against them.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (165) of all 65 Rock-type Pokémon.

Name: Shieldon
Bio: Much remains unknown about this Pokémon, as few have ever seen it. However, we know that it is calm and dislikes conflict, and it enjoys polishing its face against trees and rocks.
Fact: A Pokémon that lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its facial hide is extremely hard.

Name: Bastiodon
Bio: Its face is sturdy—as strong as diamond—and this hardness offers a very stable defense. Much about this species is still unknown, such as its natural habitat.
Fact: Any frontal attack is repulsed. It is a docile Pokémon that feeds on grass and berries.

Name: Burmy
Bio: If its cloak is even slightly damaged, Burmy will immediately repair it with whatever is close at hand. The Pokémon within the cloak is scrawny and vulnerable to the cold.
Fact: To shelter itself from cold, wintry winds, it covers itself with a cloak made of twigs and leaves.

Name: Wormadam
Bio: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.
Fact: When BURMY evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Mothim
Bio: Scatters steel-colored scales as it flaps its wings. Despite being observed feeding primarily on the nectar of flowers, Mothim is not often seen around flower gardens.
Fact: It loves the honey of flowers and steals honey collected by COMBEE.

Name: Combee
Bio: They swear fealty to a queen Pokémon and work diligently to gather nectar. Each swarm produces a different flavor of honey.
Fact: A Pokémon formed by three others. It busily carries sweet floral honey to VESPIQUEN.

Name: Vespiquen
Bio: Commands its subjects to build its hive. It will dispatch any interlopers who dare sneak into its nest and use them as nourishment for itself.
Fact: Its abdomen is a honeycomb for grubs. It raises its grubs on honey collected by COMBEE.

Name: Pachirisu
Bio: A species related to the Pikachu line. Though Pachirisu is a calm Pokémon, it still presents a danger should one touch its electrified tail or cheeks.
Fact: It makes fur balls that crackle with static electricity. It stores them with berries in tree holes.

Name: Buizel
Bio: It moves freely in the water by spinning its forked tail for propulsion. The resemblance to the screw of a steamboat is coincidental.
Fact: It has a flotation sac that is like an inflatable collar. It floats on water with its head out.

Name: Floatzel
Bio: Has a long, rather splendid flotation sac, which prevents Floatzel from drowning even in stormy seas. One might glimpse this species around fishing hamlets from time to time.
Fact: It floats using its well-developed flotation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

Name: Cherubi
Bio: Once the fruit growing alongside the main body is large and plump, Cherubi will use the nutrients within to evolve. The fruit then detaches, becoming nourishment for other creatures.
Fact: The small ball holds the nutrients needed for evolution. Apparently, it is very sweet and tasty.

Name: Cherrim
Bio: Motionless, save for the occasional quiver. A rich array of Pokémon can be found gathered around it, drawn by the scent exuded from Cherrim's folded petals.
Fact: It blooms during times of strong sunlight. It tries to make up for everything it endured as a bud.

Name: Shellos
Bio: Found in abundance on seashores bordering warm waters. Shellos are unexpectedly friendly and will crawl toward any person they see. Take care not to get coated in mucus!
Fact: Its colors and shapes differ from region to region. In the Sinnoh region, two types are confirmed.

Name: Gastrodon
Bio: Eats beach sand for nourishment. Should one Gastrodon encounter another of a different color, a fierce battle will inevitably ensue.
Fact: It has a pliable body without any bones. If any part of its body is torn off, it grows right back.

Name: Ambipom
Bio: To affirm their kinship, members of this species will form a ring by linking their newly doubled tails together. On rare occasions, humans have been accepted into such rings.
Fact: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

Name: Drifloon
Bio: Said to lure away young children and carry them off to the afterlife. Some whisper that Drifloon are formed of reincarnated human souls, but these rumors are as yet unconfirmed.
Fact: A Pokémon formed by the spirits of people and Pokémon. It loves damp, humid seasons.

Name: Drifblim
Bio: It drifts along at dusk, perfectly silent. Its transient, melancholy aspect touches some people deeply—every so often, one will come upon a song or poem devoted to Drifblim.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (150) of all 102 Flying-type Pokémon.

Name: Buneary
Bio: My hypothesis as to why Buneary rolls up its ears is that its hearing is far too keen. I surmise that the Pokémon protects its hearing by limiting the sound that may enter its ears.
Fact: It slams foes by sharply uncoiling its rolled ears. It stings enough to make a grown-up cry in pain.

Name: Lopunny
Bio: Its fur is warm and yet remarkably light. This Pokémon kicks as though it were a master of karate, driving back its opponents with ease.
Fact: An extremely cautious Pokémon. It cloaks its body with its fluffy ear fur when it senses danger.

Name: Mismagius
Bio: The incantations Mismagius chants can ward against misfortune, so a custom exists of inviting it into one's home. Incur the Pokémon's displeasure, however, and disaster will surely ensue.
Fact: Its cries sound like incantations. Those hearing it are tormented by headaches and hallucinations.

Name: Honchkrow
Bio: One cry from this Pokémon, and a murder of Murkrow come flying. At such times, one would think the curtain of night had fallen, plunging the world into jet-black darkness.
Fact: Becoming active at night, it is known to swarm with numerous MURKROW in tow.

Name: Glameow
Bio: Bewitches humans with its helical tail and piercing gaze. Its hidden claws are quite sharp as well, making this Pokémon an exceedingly tricky opponent if antagonized.
Fact: It claws if displeased and purrs when affectionate. Its fickleness is very popular among some.

Name: Purugly
Bio: Though impudent and difficult to tame, Purugly enjoys great popularity due to its fur, the beauty of which surpasses even velveteen.
Fact: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon’s nest and claims it as its own.

Name: Chingling
Bio: This Pokémon gave me an excruciating headache when it seemingly cried out without making a sound. Perhaps there are some sounds that the human ear is simply incapable of hearing.
Fact: It emits cries by agitating an orb at the back of its throat. It moves with flouncing hops.

Name: Stunky
Bio: The poison that gushes from its aft end is accompanied by an utterly evil-smelling odor with such potency that one whiff can induce memory loss.
Fact: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

Name: Skuntank
Bio: Sprays a poisonous fluid to take down prey. Sometimes, unable to stomach the stench of its own fluid, it leaves the bested prey uneaten.
Fact: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

Name: Bronzor
Bio: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.
Fact: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

Name: Bronzong
Bio: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.
Fact: One caused a news sensation when it was dug up at a construction site after a 2,000-year sleep.

Name: Bonsly
Bio: Its tears elicit sympathy from those who see them, but do not be deceived! This expulsion of body water is merely a physiological mechanism for keeping itself in good health.
Fact: It looks as if it is always crying. It is actually adjusting its body’s fluid levels by eliminating excess.

Name: Mime Jr.
Bio: Known to turn up in bustling marketplaces now and again. It mimics people much as a child would, then watches how they react, eyes sparkling.
Fact: It habitually mimics foes. Once mimicked, the foe cannot take its eyes off this Pokémon.

Name: Happiny
Bio: In imitation of Chansey, it keeps a round stone tucked into its belly pouch and cherishes it dearly. It gets along well with children and will sometimes play house with them for fun.
Fact: It loves round white things. It carries an egg-shaped rock in imitation of CHANSEY.

Name: Chatot
Bio: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.
Fact: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

Name: Spiritomb
Bio: It lays curses by thinking wicked thoughts. Writings tell that this Pokémon was born out of the assembly of five score and eight malevolent spirits.
Fact: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

Name: Gible
Bio: It nests in caves untouched by sunlight. Its sharp teeth may fall out when worn away or after an impact, but they regrow within a few days.
Fact: It nests in small, horizontal holes in cave walls. It pounces to catch prey that stray too close.

Name: Gabite
Bio: Though Gabite are usually of a violent disposition, when I gave one a glass bead it had been eyeing covetously, it suddenly became quite docile.
Fact: There is a long-held belief that medicine made from its scales will heal even incurable illnesses.

Name: Garchomp
Bio: Soars across the heavens at blinding speed—a magnificent sight! It has a feral disposition. Utmost caution is required if one meets a Garchomp out in the wilds.
Fact: When it folds up its body and extends its wings, it looks like a jet plane. It flies at sonic speed.

Name: Munchlax
Bio: Its robust stomach allows it to nonchalantly devour even rotted matter. It pays frequent visits to villages, seeking out food scraps intended for compost.
Fact: It wolfs down its weight in food once a day, swallowing food whole with almost no chewing.

Name: Riolu
Bio: Though infantile in appearance, it has the mysterious ability to read the minds of humans. The pure of heart are met with Riolu's approval, while those of ill nature earn only its loathing.
Fact: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

Name: Lucario
Bio: A most gallant-looking creature. It emits energy waves and controls them with precision, using them to sense even faraway beings. I have given the name ”aura” to this power.
Fact: It has the ability to sense the auras of all things. It understands human speech.

Name: Hippopotas
Bio: Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.
Fact: It lives in arid places. Instead of perspiration, it expels grainy sand from its body.

Name: Hippowdon
Bio: Short-tempered and easily moved to violence. It whips up whirlwinds of sand to crush its foes' spirits, then goes in for the attack.
Fact: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

Name: Skorupi
Bio: Its claws are not only razor-sharp but poisonous, making Skorupi a highly dangerous Pokémon. It seems to be weakened by cold temperatures, however.
Fact: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

Name: Drapion
Bio: Has a brutish, ferocious temperament. With immense strength and a sturdy shell off which swords will bounce, it rampages about and wreaks havoc.
Fact: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

Name: Croagunk
Bio: A poison wielder with a dastardly personality. Despite such qualities, this species is afforded a measure of popularity due to its peculiar cry and comical features.
Fact: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

Name: Toxicroak
Bio: Its crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (106) of all 69 Poison-type Pokémon.

Name: Carnivine
Bio: Though this is a plant Pokémon, it has a gluttonous and unruly temperament. Carnivine attacks its prey with its cavernous maw wide open.
Fact: It attracts prey with its sweet- smelling saliva, then chomps down. It takes a whole day to eat prey.

Name: Finneon
Bio: What a gorgeous sight this Pokémon is as it swims with its long, pink-painted caudal fins fluttering behind it. Finneon's beautiful appearance has led to its nickname: ”finery fish.”
Fact: After long exposure to sunlight, the patterns on its tail fins shine vividly when darkness arrives.

Name: Lumineon
Bio: Uses its gleaming fins to hunt its prey. The view of Lumineon schooling near the surface of the sea at night is breathtaking— it's as though there were shining stars right there.
Fact: It lives on the deep-sea floor. It attracts prey by flashing the patterns on its four tail fins.

Name: Mantyke
Bio: Though ball-like in shape, this Pokémon is a proficient swimmer. I have discovered that if a Mantyke spends much time with schools of Remoraid, it will eventually achieve evolution.
Fact: A friendly Pokémon that captures the subtle flows of seawater using its two antennae.

Name: Snover
Bio: One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.
Fact: It lives on snowy mountains. Having had little contact with humans, it is boldly inquisitive.

Name: Abomasnow
Bio: A powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.
Fact: It whips up blizzards in mountains that are always buried in snow. It is the abominable snowman.

Name: Weavile
Bio: This species corners prey as a pack, under the guidance of a leader. Weavile displays increased cunning, leading me to speculate that its evolution caused further brain development.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (125) of all 41 Ice-type Pokémon.

Name: Magnezone
Bio: I theorize that a special magnetic field influenced this Pokémon, changing its molecular structure and causing it to evolve. It emits strange radio waves toward space from its antenna.
Fact: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

Name: Lickilicky
Bio: Its tongue can extend and contract freely, and it is capable of reaching lengths over 10 times Lickilicky's height. Beware of the saliva, as it contains corrosive elements.
Fact: It wraps things with its extensible tongue. Getting too close to it will leave you soaked with drool.

Name: Rhyperior
FirstLocation: Potbottom Desert (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: This Pokémon evolved through use of a curious item. Its rocklike hide is composed of a mysterious substance and can withstand a blow from a masterwork sword with nary a scratch.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (115) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Tangrowth
Bio: Draped with long vines, it resembles a shrub in appearance. It swings bundles of vines as though they were arms, wrapping them around prey to ensnare them.
Fact: It ensnares prey by extending arms made of vines. Losing arms to predators does not trouble it.

Name: Electivire
Bio: Its evolution was induced by an unusual item, and its electrical output rises along with its heart rate. From its tails, it can unleash an electric current measuring 20,000 volts.
Fact: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

Name: Magmortar
Bio: Use of a strange item caused this Pokémon to evolve. Fireballs launched from the ends of its tubelike arms are hot enough to melt an iron pot in an instant.
Fact: It blasts fireballs of over 3,600 degrees F from the ends of its arms. It lives in volcanic craters.

Name: Togekiss
Bio: Scant few have ever sighted this Pokémon. After studying what literature remains, I am certain Togekiss will reveal itself when peace reigns in the land.
Fact: It will never appear where there is strife. Its sightings have become rare recently.

Name: Yanmega
Bio: Extremely violent. When hunting, it wastes none of its energy, aiming only for prey's most vulnerable spots. Any who manage to tame this Pokémon must be of incredible bravery.
Fact: By churning its wings, it creates shock waves that inflict critical internal injuries to foes.

Name: Leafeon
Bio: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.
Fact: Just like a plant, it uses photosynthesis. As a result, it is always enveloped in clear air.

Name: Glaceon
Bio: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.
Fact: As a protective technique, it can completely freeze its fur to make its hairs stand like needles.

Name: Gliscor
Bio: It glides soundlessly on pitch-black wings and sinks sharp fangs into the throat of its prey. It takes on a look of satisfaction once it has entirely drained its prey of blood.
Fact: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

Name: Mamoswine
Bio: This species reached its zenith during the period known as the ice age. I suspect that Hisui's frigid climate is in harmony with Mamoswine's constitution, thus awakening hidden potential.
Fact: Its impressive tusks are made of ice. The population thinned when it turned warm after the ice age.

Name: Porygon-Z
Bio: A curious item induced this evolution. The Pokémon's offensive capabilities have greatly increased, but the strangeness of its behavior has magnified in equal measure. This worries me.
Fact: Additional software was installed to make it a better Pokémon. It began acting oddly, however.

Name: Gallade
Bio: The blades extending from its elbows are sharper than the finest swords. Its swordsmanship, albeit self-taught, is astonishingly impressive.
Fact: A master of courtesy and swordsmanship, it fights using extending swords on its elbows.

Name: Probopass
Bio: It is able to emit powerful magnetism, allowing it control over the iron sand that forms its luscious mustache. Using this iron sand, Probopass forms hard stones with which it smites its prey.
Fact: It exudes strong magnetism from all over. It controls three small units called Mini-Noses.

Name: Dusknoir
Bio: Comes to those whose lives have come to an end and escorts their souls to the afterlife. Known to mistakenly take the souls of those who yet have life left in them, albeit rarely.
Fact: The antenna on its head captures radio waves from the world of spirits that command it to take people there.

Name: Froslass
Bio: A Pokémon inhabited by the soul of a woman who died bearing a grudge in the snowy mountains. Legends of Froslass placing deathly curses on misbehaving men send shivers down my spine.
Fact: The only Ice/Ghost type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Rotom
Bio: This bizarre Pokémon appears to be a will-o'-the-wisp powered by electricity. Be wary, as Rotom is both smart and mischievous.
Fact: The only Electric/Ghost type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Uxie
Bio: A Pokémon feared but also respected for stealing away the memories of evildoers. I have found records that suggest Uxie holds dominion over knowledge.
Fact: Known as “The Being of Knowledge.” It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

Name: Mesprit
Bio: Known as the Being of Emotion. In legend, this Pokémon was feared, as any who showed disrespect would have their emotions thrown into disarray.
Fact: Known as “The Being of Emotion.” It taught humans the nobility of sorrow, pain, and joy.

Name: Azelf
Bio: The dreaded Being of Willpower. Legends tell of this Pokémon manipulating the will of its adversaries and turning them into puppets of its own.
Fact: Known as “The Being of Willpower.” It sleeps at the bottom of a lake to keep the world in balance.

Name: Dialga
Bio: This Pokémon is revered as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Dialga was what caused the vast river of time to begin flowing in our world.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (150) of all 54 Steel-type Pokémon.

Name: Palkia
Bio: This Pokémon is feared as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Palkia was what caused the walls of our world to disappear, creating a sky that spans for infinity.
Fact: Has the highest base total base stats (680) of all 141 Water-type Pokémon.

Name: Heatran
Bio: Stories tell of this Pokémon being birthed from the boiling magma within Mount Coronet. Its molten-steel body holds many mysteries.
Fact: The only Fire/Steel type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Regigigas
Bio: According to legend, Regigigas pulled landmasses together and bound them with rope to create the continent of Hisui. Though I have my doubts, the story could well contain a shred of truth.
Fact: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

Name: Giratina
Bio: There is one Hisuian verse that tells of a powerful light creating a deep shadow. I imagine that this deep shadow is Giratina.
Fact: A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.

Name: Cresselia
Bio: Cresselia is reminiscent of the crescent moon. It leaves a brilliant line of light in its wake as it flies across the night sky. I daresay it resembles the heavenly maiden who created the Milky Way.
Fact: Shiny particles are released from its wings like a veil. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Phione
Bio: Can be seen floating offshore during seasons when the seas are warm. Its azure body blends in with the ocean waters—logic suggests this is a defense mechanism against natural predators.
Fact: A Pokémon that lives in warm seas. It inflates the flotation sac on its head to drift and search for food.

Name: Manaphy
Bio: Rumored to migrate across the oceans and visit Hisui's coastal waters only rarely. Although Manaphy resembles Phione, it is also quite different. The relation between the two is unclear.
Fact: Born on a cold seafloor, it will swim great distances to return to its birthplace.

Name: Darkrai
Bio: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (135) of all 55 Dark-type Pokémon.

Name: Shaymin
Bio: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.
Fact: It lives in flower patches and avoids detection by curling up to look like a flowering plant.

Name: Arceus
Bio: It is the heavenly fount from which pours the light that shines across Hisui. Its luminance guides and protects all Pokémon. Hisuian mythology states that Arceus is the creator of all things.
Fact: Has the highest base total base stats (720) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Victini
Bio: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.
Fact: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Snivy
Bio: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.
Fact: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

Name: Servine
Bio: When it gets dirty, its leaves can’t be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.
Fact: It moves along the ground as if sliding. Its swift movements befuddle its foes, and it then attacks with a vine whip.

Name: Serperior
Bio: It can stop its opponents’ movements with just a glare. It takes in solar energy and boosts it internally.
Fact: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give it their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

Name: Tepig
Bio: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.
Fact: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

Name: Pignite
Bio: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.
Fact: Whatever it eats becomes fuel for the flame in its stomach. When it is angered, the intensity of the flame increases.

Name: Emboar
Bio: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.
Fact: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

Name: Oshawott
Bio: This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name ”scalchop”.
Fact: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

Name: Dewott
Bio: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.
Fact: Strict training is how it learns its flowing double-scalchop technique.

Name: Samurott
Bio: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.
Fact: One swing of the sword incorporated in its armor can fell an opponent. A simple glare from one of them quiets everybody.

Name: Patrat
Bio: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.
Fact: Using food stored in cheek pouches, they can keep watch for days. They use their tails to communicate with others.

Name: Watchog
Bio: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.
Fact: They make the patterns on their bodies shine in order to threaten predators. Keen eyesight lets them see in the dark.

Name: Lillipup
Bio: This Pokémon is far brighter than the average child, and Lillipup won’t forget the love it receives or any abuse it suffers.
Fact: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

Name: Herdier
Bio: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.
Fact: It has black, cape-like fur that is very hard and decreases the amount of damage it receives.

Name: Stoutland
Bio: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.
Fact: It rescues people stranded by blizzards in the mountains. Its shaggy fur shields it from the cold.

Name: Purrloin
Bio: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.
Fact: They steal from people for fun, but their victims can’t help but forgive them. Their deceptively cute act is perfect.

Name: Liepard
Bio: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.
Fact: These Pokémon vanish and appear unexpectedly. Many Trainers are drawn to their beautiful form and fur.

Name: Pansage
Bio: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.
Fact: This Pokémon dwells deep in the forest. Eating a leaf from its head whisks weariness away as if by magic.

Name: Simisage
Bio: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.
Fact: It attacks enemies with strikes of its thorn-covered tail. This Pokémon is wild tempered.

Name: Pansear
Bio: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: When it is angered, the temperature of its head tuft reaches 600° F. It uses its tuft to roast berries.

Name: Simisear
Bio: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.
Fact: It loves sweets because they become energy for the fire burning inside its body.

Name: Panpour
Bio: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.
Fact: It does not thrive in dry environments. It keeps itself damp by shooting water stored in its head tuft from its tail.

Name: Simipour
Bio: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.
Fact: The tuft on its head holds water. When the level runs low, it replenishes the tuft by siphoning up water with its tail.

Name: Munna
Bio: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it’s eating a good dream, and black when it’s eating a nightmare.
Fact: Munna always float in the air. People whose dreams are eaten by them forget what the dreams had been about.

Name: Musharna
Bio: It drowses and dreams all the time. It’s best to leave it be if it’s just woken up, as it’s a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.
Fact: The mist emanating from their foreheads is packed with the dreams of people and Pokémon.

Name: Pidove
Bio: It’s forgetful and not very bright, but many Trainers love it anyway for its friendliness and sincerity.
Fact: Each follows its Trainer’s orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

Name: Tranquill
Bio: These bright Pokémon have acute memories. Apparently delivery workers often choose them as their partners.
Fact: It can return to its Trainer’s location regardless of the distance separating them.

Name: Unfezant
Bio: This Pokémon is intelligent and intensely proud. People will sit up and take notice if you become the Trainer of one.
Fact: Males swing their head plumage to threaten opponents. The females’ flying abilities surpass those of the males.

Name: Blitzle
Bio: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.
Fact: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

Name: Zebstrika
Bio: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.
Fact: This ill-tempered Pokémon is dangerous because when it’s angry, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

Name: Roggenrola
Bio: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.
Fact: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

Name: Boldore
Bio: It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.
Fact: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

Name: Gigalith
Bio: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.
Fact: Compressing the energy from its internal core lets it fire off an attack capable of blowing away a mountain.

Name: Woobat
Bio: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.
Fact: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

Name: Swoobat
FirstLocation: Challenge Beach (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.
Fact: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

Name: Drilbur
Bio: It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.
Fact: It can dig through the ground at a speed of 30 mph. It could give a car running aboveground a good race.

Name: Excadrill
Bio: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.
Fact: It can help in tunnel construction. Its drill has evolved into steel strong enough to bore through iron plates.

Name: Audino
Bio: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.
Fact: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

Name: Timburr
Bio: Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.
Fact: It fights by swinging a piece of lumber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

Name: Gurdurr
Bio: Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there’s skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.
Fact: This Pokémon is so muscular and strongly built that even a group of wrestlers could not make it budge an inch.

Name: Conkeldurr
Bio: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (140) of all 61 Fighting-type Pokémon.

Name: Tympole
Bio: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.
Fact: They warn others of danger by vibrating their cheeks to create a high-pitched sound.

Name: Palpitoad
Bio: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.
Fact: When they vibrate the bumps on their heads, they can make waves in water or earthquake-like vibrations on land.

Name: Seismitoad
Bio: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.
Fact: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

Name: Throh
Bio: They train in groups of five. Any member that can’t keep up will discard its belt and leave the group.
Fact: When it tightens its belt, it becomes stronger. Wild Throh use vines to weave their own belts.

Name: Sawk
Bio: The karate chops of a Sawk that’s trained itself to the limit can cleave the ocean itself.
Fact: The sound of Sawk punching boulders and trees can be heard all the way from the mountains where they train.

Name: Sewaddle
Bio: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.
Fact: Leavanny dress it in clothes they made for it when it hatched. It hides its head in its hood while it is sleeping.

Name: Swadloon
Bio: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.
Fact: Forests where Swadloon live have superb foliage because the nutrients they make from fallen leaves nourish the plant life.

Name: Leavanny
Bio: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.
Fact: Upon finding a small Pokémon, it weaves clothing for it from leaves, using the cutters on its arms and sticky silk.

Name: Venipede
Bio: Its fangs are highly venomous. If this Pokémon finds prey it thinks it can eat, it leaps for them without any thought of how things might turn out.
Fact: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

Name: Whirlipede
Bio: Whirlipede protects itself with a sturdy shell and poisonous spikes while it stores up the energy it’ll need for evolution.
Fact: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

Name: Scolipede
Bio: Scolipede engage in fierce territorial battles with Centiskorch. At the end of one of these battles, the victor makes a meal of the loser.
Fact: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

Name: Cottonee
Bio: Weaving together the cotton of both Cottonee and Eldegoss produces exquisite cloth that’s highly prized by many luxury brands.
Fact: When attacked, it escapes by shooting cotton from its body. The cotton serves as a decoy to distract the attacker.

Name: Whimsicott
Bio: As long as this Pokémon bathes in sunlight, its cotton keeps growing. If too much cotton fluff builds up, Whimsicott tears it off and scatters it.
Fact: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

Name: Petilil
Bio: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.
Fact: The leaves on its head are very bitter. Eating one of these leaves is known to refresh a tired body.

Name: Lilligant
Bio: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.
Fact: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

Name: Basculin
Bio: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.
Fact: Red and blue Basculin get along so poorly, they’ll start fighting instantly. These Pokémon are very hostile.

Name: Sandile
Bio: Sandile is small, but its legs and lower body are powerful. Pushing sand aside as it goes, Sandile moves through the desert as if it’s swimming.
Fact: They live buried in the sands of the desert. The sun-warmed sands prevent their body temperature from dropping.

Name: Krokorok
Bio: Although this Pokémon has specialized eyes that allow it to see in the dark, Krokorok won’t move much at night—the desert gets cold after sunset.
Fact: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

Name: Krookodile
FirstLocation: Potbottom Desert (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: While terribly aggressive, Krookodile also has the patience to stay hidden under sand for days, lying in wait for prey.
Fact: They never allow prey to escape. Their jaws are so powerful, they can crush the body of an automobile.

Name: Darumaka
Bio: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.
Fact: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

Name: Darmanitan
Bio: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (140) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Maractus
Bio: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They’re jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.
Fact: It uses an up-tempo song and dance to drive away the bird Pokémon that prey on its flower seeds.

Name: Dwebble
Bio: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.
Fact: This Pokémon can easily melt holes in hard rocks with a liquid secreted from its mouth.

Name: Crustle
Bio: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They’re mighty enough to crack Rhyperior’s carapace.
Fact: Competing for territory, Crustle fight viciously. The one whose boulder is broken is the loser of the battle.

Name: Scraggy
Bio: It protects itself with its durable skin. It’s thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.
Fact: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

Name: Scrafty
Bio: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (115) of all 55 Dark-type Pokémon.

Name: Sigilyph
Bio: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.
Fact: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

Name: Yamask
Bio: The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.
Fact: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

Name: Cofagrigus
Bio: There are many depictions of Cofagrigus decorating ancient tombs. They’re symbols of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had.
Fact: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

Name: Tirtouga
Bio: Tirtouga is considered to be the ancestor of many turtle Pokémon. It was restored to life from a fossil.
Fact: Restored from a fossil, this Pokémon can dive to depths beyond half a mile.

Name: Carracosta
Bio: This Pokémon emerges from the water in search of prey despite the fact that it moves more slowly on land.
Fact: They can live both in the ocean and on land. A slap from one of them is enough to open a hole in the bottom of a tanker.

Name: Archen
Bio: Archen is said to be the ancestor of bird Pokémon. It lived in treetops, eating berries and bug Pokémon.
Fact: Said to be an ancestor of bird Pokémon, they were unable to fly and moved about by hopping from one branch to another.

Name: Archeops
Bio: Though capable of flight, Archeops was apparently better at hunting on the ground.
Fact: They are intelligent and will cooperate to catch prey. From the ground, they use a running start to take flight.

Name: Trubbish
Bio: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.
Fact: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

Name: Garbodor
Bio: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.
Fact: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

Name: Zorua
Bio: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.
Fact: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.

Name: Zoroark
Bio: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.
Fact: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Minccino
Bio: They pet each other with their tails as a form of greeting. Of the two, the one whose tail is fluffier is a bit more boastful.
Fact: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

Name: Cinccino
