Bio: A special oil that seeps through their fur helps them avoid attacks. The oil fetches a high price at market.
Fact: Their white fur is coated in a special oil that makes it easy for them to deflect attacks.

Name: Gothita
Bio: Even when nobody seems to be around, Gothita can still be heard making a muted cry. Many believe it’s speaking to something only it can see.
Fact: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

Name: Gothorita
Bio: On nights when the stars shine, this Pokémon’s psychic power is at its strongest. It’s unknown just what link Gothorita has to the greater universe.
Fact: They use hypnosis to control people and Pokémon. Tales of Gothorita leading people astray are told in every corner.

Name: Gothitelle
Bio: A criminal who was shown his fate by a Gothitelle went missing that same day and was never seen again.
Fact: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

Name: Solosis
Bio: Many say that the special liquid covering this Pokémon’s body would allow it to survive in the vacuum of space.
Fact: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

Name: Duosion
Bio: Its brain has split into two, and the two halves rarely think alike. Its actions are utterly unpredictable.
Fact: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

Name: Reuniclus
Bio: It’s said that drinking the liquid surrounding Reuniclus grants wisdom. Problem is, the liquid is highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.
Fact: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

Name: Ducklett
Bio: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.
Fact: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food--peat moss.

Name: Swanna
Bio: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.
Fact: It administers sharp, powerful pecks with its bill. It whips its long neck to deliver forceful repeated strikes.

Name: Vanillite
Bio: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.
Fact: The temperature of their breath is -58° F. They create snow crystals and make snow fall in the areas around them.

Name: Vanillish
Bio: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.
Fact: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon’s habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

Name: Vanilluxe
Bio: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and attack their foes with violent blizzards.

Name: Deerling
Bio: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.
Fact: The color and scent of their fur changes to match the mountain grass. When they sense hostility, they hide in the grass.

Name: Sawsbuck
Bio: They migrate according to the seasons, so some people call Sawsbuck the harbingers of spring.
Fact: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

Name: Emolga
Bio: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.
Fact: The energy made in its cheeks’ electric pouches is stored inside its membrane and released while it is gliding.

Name: Karrablast
Bio: It spits a liquid from its mouth to melt through Shelmet’s shell. Karrablast doesn’t eat the shell— it eats only the contents.
Fact: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

Name: Escavalier
FirstLocation: Forest of Focus (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: It charges its enemies, lances at the ready. An image of one of its duels is captured in a famous painting of Escavalier clashing with Sirfetch’d.
Fact: They fly around at high speed, striking with their pointed spears. Even when in trouble, they face opponents bravely.

Name: Foongus
Bio: The spores released from this Pokémon’s hands are highly poisonous, but when thoroughly dried, the spores can be used as stomach medicine.
Fact: It lures people in with its Poké Ball pattern, then releases poison spores. Why it resembles a Poké Ball is unknown.

Name: Amoonguss
Bio: Amoonguss generally doesn’t move much. It tends to stand still near Poké Balls that have been dropped on the ground.
Fact: It lures prey close by dancing and waving its arm caps, which resemble Poké Balls, in a swaying motion.

Name: Frillish
Bio: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.
Fact: With its thin, veil-like arms wrapped around the body of its opponent, it sinks to the ocean floor.

Name: Jellicent
Bio: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.
Fact: The fate of the ships and crew that wander into Jellicent’s habitat: all sunken, all lost, all vanished.

Name: Alomomola
Bio: The reason it helps Pokémon in a weakened condition is that any Pokémon coming after them may also attack Alomomola.
Fact: The special membrane enveloping Alomomola has the ability to heal wounds.

Name: Joltik
Bio: Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They’re often found sticking to Yamper’s hindquarters.
Fact: Tied for the smallest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 m.

Name: Galvantula
Bio: It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are not yet good at flying.
Fact: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

Name: Ferroseed
Bio: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.
Fact: When threatened, it attacks by shooting a barrage of spikes, which gives it a chance to escape by rolling away.

Name: Ferrothorn
Bio: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.
Fact: It fights by swinging around its three spiky feelers. A hit from these steel spikes can reduce a boulder to rubble.

Name: Klink
Bio: It’s suspected that Klink were the inspiration behind ancient people’s invention of the first gears.
Fact: The two minigears that mesh together are predetermined. Each will rebound from other minigears without meshing.

Name: Klang
Bio: Many companies in the Galar region choose Klang as their logo. This Pokémon is considered the symbol of industrial technology.
Fact: By changing the direction in which it rotates, it communicates its feelings to others. When angry, it rotates faster.

Name: Klinklang
Bio: The three gears that compose this Pokémon spin at high speed. Its new spiked gear isn’t a living creature.
Fact: Its red core functions as an energy tank. It fires the charged energy through its spikes into an area.

Name: Tynamo
Bio: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.
Fact: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

Name: Eelektrik
Bio: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.
Fact: They coil around foes and shock them with electricity-generating organs that seem simply to be circular patterns.

Name: Eelektross
Bio: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.
Fact: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

Name: Elgyem
Bio: This Pokémon was discovered about 50 years ago. Its highly developed brain enables it to exert its psychic powers.
Fact: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

Name: Beheeyem
Bio: Sometimes found drifting above wheat fields, this Pokémon can control the memories of its opponents.
Fact: It can manipulate an opponent’s memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

Name: Litwick
Bio: The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns.
Fact: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

Name: Lampent
Bio: It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them.
Fact: This ominous Pokémon is feared. Through cities it wanders, searching for the spirits of the fallen.

Name: Chandelure
Bio: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said.
Fact: It absorbs a spirit, which it then burns. By waving the flames on its arms, it puts its foes into a hypnotic trance.

Name: Axew
Bio: They play with each other by knocking their large tusks together. Their tusks break sometimes, but they grow back so quickly that it isn’t a concern.
Fact: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

Name: Fraxure
Bio: Its skin is as hard as a suit of armor. Fraxure’s favorite strategy is to tackle its opponents, stabbing them with its tusks at the same time.
Fact: Since a broken tusk will not grow back, they diligently sharpen their tusks on river rocks after they’ve been fighting.

Name: Haxorus
Bio: While usually kindhearted, it can be terrifying if angered. Tusks that can slice through steel beams are how Haxorus deals with its adversaries.
Fact: They are kind but can be relentless when defending territory. They challenge foes with tusks that can cut steel.

Name: Cubchoo
Bio: It sniffles before performing a move, using its frosty snot to provide an icy element to any move that needs it.
Fact: When it is not feeling well, its mucus gets watery and the power of its Ice-type moves decreases.

Name: Beartic
Bio: It swims energetically through frigid seas. When it gets tired, it freezes the seawater with its breath so it can rest on the ice.
Fact: It can make its breath freeze at will. Very able in the water, it swims around in northern seas and catches prey.

Name: Cryogonal
Bio: When the weather gets hot, these Pokémon turn into water vapor. Cryogonal are almost never seen during summer.
Fact: When its body temperature goes up, it turns into steam and vanishes. When its temperature lowers, it returns to ice.

Name: Shelmet
Bio: It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.
Fact: When attacked, it defends itself by closing the lid of its shell. It can spit a sticky, poisonous liquid.

Name: Accelgor
FirstLocation: Forest of Focus (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.
Fact: When its body dries out, it weakens. So, to prevent dehydration, it wraps itself in many layers of thin membrane.

Name: Stunfisk
Bio: For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.
Fact: The only Ground/Electric type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Mienfoo
Bio: Though small, Mienfoo’s temperament is fierce. Any creature that approaches Mienfoo carelessly will be greeted with a flurry of graceful attacks.
Fact: In fights, they dominate with onslaughts of flowing, continuous attacks. With their sharp claws, they cut enemies.

Name: Mienshao
Bio: Delivered at blinding speeds, kicks from this Pokémon can shatter massive boulders into tiny pieces.
Fact: It wields the fur on its arms like a whip. Its arm attacks come with such rapidity that they cannot even be seen.

Name: Druddigon
Bio: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.
Fact: It warms its body by absorbing sunlight with its wings. When its body temperature falls, it can no longer move.

Name: Golett
Bio: This Pokémon was created from clay. It received orders from its master many thousands of years ago, and it still follows those orders to this day.
Fact: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

Name: Golurk
Bio: There’s a theory that inside Golurk is a perpetual motion machine that produces limitless energy, but this belief hasn’t been proven.
Fact: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

Name: Pawniard
Bio: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.
Fact: Blades comprise this Pokémon’s entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river.

Name: Bisharp
Bio: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.
Fact: It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses.

Name: Bouffalant
Bio: These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason.
Fact: Their fluffy fur absorbs damage, even if they strike foes with a fierce headbutt.

Name: Rufflet
Bio: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.
Fact: They crush berries with their talons. They bravely stand up to any opponent, no matter how strong it is.

Name: Braviary
Bio: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.
Fact: They fight for their friends without any thought about danger to themselves. One can carry a car while flying.

Name: Vullaby
Bio: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.
Fact: Its wings are too tiny to allow it to fly. As the time approaches for it to evolve, it discards the bones it was wearing.

Name: Mandibuzz
Bio: They adorn themselves with bones. There seem to be fashion trends among them, as different bones come into and fall out of popularity.
Fact: It makes a nest out of bones it finds. It grabs weakened prey in its talons and hauls it to its nest of bones.

Name: Heatmor
Bio: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.
Fact: It breathes through a hole in its tail while it burns with an internal fire. Durant is its prey.

Name: Durant
Bio: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.
Fact: They attack in groups, covering themselves in steel armor to protect themselves from Heatmor.

Name: Deino
Bio: Because it can’t see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.
Fact: It tends to bite everything, and it is not a picky eater. Approaching it carelessly is dangerous.

Name: Zweilous
Bio: Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.
Fact: After it has eaten up all the food in its territory, it moves to another area. Its two heads do not get along.

Name: Hydreigon
Bio: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won’t slow until their target goes down.
Fact: This brutal Pokémon travels the skies on its six wings. Anything that moves seems like a foe to it, triggering its attack.

Name: Larvesta
Bio: Larvesta’s body is warm all over. It spouts fire from the tips of its horns to intimidate predators and scare prey.
Fact: This Pokémon was believed to have been born from the sun. When it evolves, its entire body is engulfed in flames.

Name: Volcarona
Bio: This Pokémon emerges from a cocoon formed of raging flames. Ancient murals depict Volcarona as a deity of fire.
Fact: When volcanic ash darkened the atmosphere, it is said that Volcarona’s fire provided a replacement for the sun.

Name: Cobalion
Bio: From the moment it’s born, this Pokémon radiates the air of a leader. Its presence will calm even vicious foes.
Fact: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

Name: Terrakion
Bio: In Unovan legend, Terrakion battled against humans in an effort to protect other Pokémon.
Fact: The only Rock/Fighting type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Virizion
Bio: It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (129) of all 106 Grass-type Pokémon.

Name: Tornadus
Bio: This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one.
Fact: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Thundurus
Bio: They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil.
Fact: Countless charred remains mar the landscape of places through which Thundurus has passed.

Name: Reshiram
Bio: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.
Fact: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Zekrom
Bio: Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom’s lightning.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (150) of all 57 Electric-type Pokémon.

Name: Landorus
Bio: When the incarnations of wind and of lightning clash, Landorus arrives to quell the conflict. After the tempests and thunderbolts abate, the land is sure to be blessed with bountiful harvests.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (115) of all 67 Ground-type Pokémon.

Name: Kyurem
Bio: It appears that this Pokémon uses its powers over ice to freeze its own body in order to stabilize its cellular structure.
Fact: The only Dragon/Ice type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Keldeo
Bio: They say that Keldeo must survive harsh battles and fully develop the horn on its forehead before this Pokémon’s true power will awaken.
Fact: By blasting water from its hooves, it can glide across water. It excels at using leg moves while battling.

Name: Meloetta
Bio: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.
Fact: Many famous songs have been inspired by the melodies that Meloetta plays.

Name: Genesect
Bio: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.
Fact: Has the highest base total base stats (600) of all 84 Bug-type Pokémon.

Name: Chespin
Bio: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn’t faze it.
Fact: The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

Name: Quilladin
Bio: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won’t start fights.
Fact: It relies on its sturdy shell to deflect predators’ attacks. It counterattacks with its sharp quills.

Name: Chesnaught
Bio: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.
Fact: Its Tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

Name: Fennekin
Bio: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.
Fact: Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Braixen
Bio: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.
Fact: It has a twig stuck in its tail. With friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

Name: Delphox
Bio: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.
Fact: It gazes into the flame at the tip of its branch to achieve a focused state, which allows it to see into the future.

Name: Froakie
Bio: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.
Fact: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

Name: Frogadier
Bio: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.
Fact: It can throw bubble-covered pebbles with precise control, hitting empty cans up to a hundred feet away.

Name: Greninja
Bio: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.
Fact: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

Name: Bunnelby
Bio: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.
Fact: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

Name: Diggersby
Bio: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.
Fact: The only Normal/Ground type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Fletchling
Bio: When this Pokémon gets excited, its body temperature increases sharply. If you touch a Fletchling with bare hands, you might get burned.
Fact: These friendly Pokémon send signals to one another with beautiful chirps and tail-feather movements.

Name: Fletchinder
Bio: Fletchinder are exceedingly territorial and aggressive. These Pokémon fight among themselves over feeding grounds.
Fact: From its beak, it expels embers that set the tall grass on fire. Then it pounces on the bewildered prey that pop out of the grass.

Name: Talonflame
Bio: Talonflame dives toward prey at speeds of up to 310 mph and assaults them with powerful kicks, giving the prey no chance to escape.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (126) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Scatterbug
Bio: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.
Fact: When under attack from bird Pokémon, it spews a poisonous black powder that causes paralysis on contact.

Name: Spewpa
Bio: The beaks of bird Pokémon can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.
Fact: It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it quickly bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

Name: Vivillon
Bio: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.
Fact: Vivillon with many different patterns are found all over the world. These patterns are affected by the climate of their habitat.

Name: Litleo
Bio: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.
Fact: The stronger the opponent it faces, the more heat surges from its mane and the more power flows through its body.

Name: Pyroar
Bio: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn’t use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.
Fact: The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

Name: Flabébé
Bio: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it’s collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.
Fact: Tied for the lightest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 kg.

Name: Floette
Bio: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.
Fact: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

Name: Florges
Bio: Its life can span several hundred years. It’s said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (154) of all 52 Fairy-type Pokémon.

Name: Skiddo
Bio: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn’t need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.
Fact: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

Name: Gogoat
Bio: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (123) of all 106 Grass-type Pokémon.

Name: Pancham
Bio: Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.
Fact: It does its best to be taken seriously by its enemies, but its glare is not sufficiently intimidating. Chewing on a leaf is its trademark.

Name: Pangoro
Bio: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.
Fact: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won’t put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

Name: Furfrou
Bio: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.
Fact: Trimming its fluffy fur not only makes it more elegant but also increases the swiftness of its movements.

Name: Espurr
Bio: There’s enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can’t be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.
Fact: The organ that emits its intense psychic power is sheltered by its ears to keep power from leaking out.

Name: Meowstic
Bio: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It’s when they’re protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.
Fact: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

Name: Honedge
Bio: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.
Fact: Apparently this Pokémon is born when a departed spirit inhabits a sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

Name: Doublade
Bio: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (150) of all 51 Ghost-type Pokémon.

Name: Aegislash
Bio: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (140) of all 51 Ghost-type Pokémon.

Name: Spritzee
Bio: The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee.
Fact: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

Name: Aromatisse
Bio: The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight.
Fact: It devises various scents, pleasant and unpleasant, and emits scents that its enemies dislike in order to gain an edge in battle.

Name: Swirlix
Bio: The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix’s fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.
Fact: To entangle its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

Name: Slurpuff
Bio: Slurpuff’s fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.
Fact: It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

Name: Inkay
Bio: By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.
Fact: Opponents who stare at the flashing of the light-emitting spots on its body become dazed and lose their will to fight.

Name: Malamar
FirstLocation: Fields of Honor (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: It’s said that Malamar’s hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.
Fact: It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

Name: Binacle
Bio: If the two don’t work well together, both their offense and defense fall apart. Without good teamwork, they won’t survive.
Fact: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

Name: Barbaracle
Bio: Having an eye on each palm allows it to keep watch in all directions. In a pinch, its limbs start to act on their own to ensure the enemy’s defeat.
Fact: When they evolve, two Binacle multiply into seven. They fight with the power of seven Binacle.

Name: Skrelp
Bio: Skrelp looks like a piece of rotten seaweed, so it can blend in with seaweed drifting on the ocean and avoid being detected by enemies.
Fact: Camouflaged as rotten kelp, they spray liquid poison on prey that approaches unawares and then finish it off.

Name: Dragalge
Bio: Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean’s surface and bathing it in sunlight.
Fact: Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

Name: Clauncher
Bio: By detonating gas that accumulates in its right claw, this Pokémon launches water like a bullet. This is how Clauncher defeats its enemies.
Fact: They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

Name: Clawitzer
Bio: Clawitzer’s right arm is a cannon that launches projectiles made of seawater. Shots from a Clawitzer’s cannon arm can sink a tanker.
Fact: Their enormous claws launch cannonballs of water powerful enough to pierce tanker hulls.

Name: Helioptile
Bio: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.
Fact: They make their home in deserts. They can generate their energy from basking in the sun, so eating food is not a requirement.

Name: Heliolisk
Bio: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.
Fact: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

Name: Tyrunt
Bio: This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around.
Fact: This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn’t like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

Name: Tyrantrum
Bio: A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.
Fact: Thanks to its gargantuan jaws, which could shred thick metal plates as if they were paper, it was invincible in the ancient world it once inhabited.

Name: Amaura
Bio: Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice.
Fact: This ancient Pokémon was restored from part of its body that had been frozen in ice for over 100 million years.

Name: Aurorus
Bio: When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (123) of all 65 Rock-type Pokémon.

Name: Sylveon
Bio: It emits a soothing aura from its ribbon-shaped organs. It wraps these appendages around quarrelers to instantly restore calm to the situation.
Fact: It sends a soothing aura from its ribbonlike feelers to calm fights.

Name: Hawlucha
Bio: It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.
Fact: The only Fighting/Flying type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Dedenne
Bio: Since Dedenne can’t generate much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.
Fact: Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

Name: Carbink
Bio: It’s said that somewhere in the world, there’s a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It’s also said that this pack has a queen.
Fact: Born from the temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head.

Name: Goomy
Bio: Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.
Fact: The weakest Dragon-type Pokémon, it lives in damp, shady places, so its body doesn’t dry out.

Name: Sliggoo
Bio: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
Fact: It drives away opponents by excreting a sticky liquid that can dissolve anything. Its eyes devolved, so it can’t see anything.

Name: Goodra
Bio: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Defense (150) of all 56 Dragon-type Pokémon.

Name: Klefki
Bio: Klefki sucks in metal ions with the horn topping its head. It seems this Pokémon loves keys so much that its head needed to look like one, too.
Fact: These key collectors threaten any attackers by fiercely jingling their keys at them.

Name: Phantump
Bio: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.
Fact: These Pokémon are created when spirits possess rotten tree stumps. They prefer to live in abandoned forests.

Name: Trevenant
Bio: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon’s feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.
Fact: It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Bio: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.
Fact: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

Name: Gourgeist
Bio: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.
Fact: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

Name: Bergmite
Bio: Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.
Fact: It blocks opponents’ attacks with the ice that shields its body. It uses cold air to repair any cracks with new ice.

Name: Avalugg
Bio: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (184) of all 41 Ice-type Pokémon.

Name: Noibat
Bio: No wavelength of sound is beyond Noibat’s ability to produce. The ultrasonic waves it generates can overcome much larger Pokémon.
Fact: They live in pitch-black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

Name: Noivern
Bio: Flying through the darkness, it weakens enemies with ultrasonic waves that could crush stone. Its fangs finish the fight.
Fact: They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

Name: Xerneas
Bio: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (131) of all 52 Fairy-type Pokémon.

Name: Yveltal
Bio: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.
Fact: Has the highest base total base stats (680) of all 55 Dark-type Pokémon.

Name: Zygarde
Bio: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.
Fact: When the Kalos region’s ecosystem falls into disarray, it appears and reveals its secret power.

Name: Diancie
Bio: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.
Fact: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

Name: Hoopa
Bio: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.
Fact: This troublemaker sends anything and everything to faraway places using its loop, which can warp space.

Name: Volcanion
Bio: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.
Fact: The only Fire/Water type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Rowlet
Bio: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.
Fact: This wary Pokémon uses photosynthesis to store up energy during the day, while becoming active at night.

Name: Dartrix
Bio: Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.
Fact: A bit of a dandy, it spends its free time preening its wings. Its preoccupation with any dirt on its plumage can leave it unable to battle.

Name: Decidueye
Bio: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.
Fact: It fires arrow quills from its wings with such precision, they can pierce a pebble at distances over a hundred yards.

Name: Litten
Bio: Trying to pet Litten before it trusts you will result in a nasty scratch from its sharp claws. Be careful.
Fact: While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

Name: Torracat
Bio: When facing a powerful enemy, Torracat’s fighting spirit gets pumped up, and its fire bell blazes hotter.
Fact: At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever this Pokémon spits fire.

Name: Incineroar
Bio: Incineroar’s rough and aggressive behavior is its most notable trait, but the way it helps out small Pokémon shows that it has a kind side as well.
Fact: This Pokémon has a violent, selfish disposition. If it’s not in the mood to listen, it will ignore its Trainer’s orders with complete nonchalance.

Name: Popplio
Bio: If Popplio want to create big, powerful balloons, they must be persistent. It takes daily practice for these Pokémon to develop their skills.
Fact: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It’s famous for being a hard worker.

Name: Brionne
Bio: On nights when the sea is calm, Brionne dance with one another to the singing of the Primarina that’s leading them.
Fact: A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards its enemies.

Name: Primarina
Bio: For Primarina, every battle’s a stage. Its singing and the dancing of its balloons will mesmerize the audience.
Fact: It controls its water balloons with song. The melody is learned from others of its kind and is passed down from one generation to the next.

Name: Pikipek
Bio: It may look spindly, but its neck muscles are heavy-duty. It can peck at a tree 16 times per second!
Fact: It can peck at a rate of 16 times a second to drill holes in trees. It uses the holes for food storage and for nesting.

Name: Trumbeak
Bio: From its mouth, it fires the seeds of berries it has eaten. The scattered seeds give rise to new plants.
Fact: It eats berries and stores their seeds in its beak. When it encounters enemies or prey, it fires off all the seeds in a burst.

Name: Toucannon
Bio: Known for forming harmonious couples, this Pokémon is brought to wedding ceremonies as a good luck charm.
Fact: When it battles, its beak heats up. The temperature can easily exceed 212 degrees Fahrenheit, causing severe burns when it hits.

Name: Yungoos
Bio: Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.
Fact: With its sharp fangs, it will bite anything. It did not originally live in Alola but was imported from another region.

Name: Gumshoos
Bio: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!
Fact: When it finds a trace of its prey, it patiently stakes out the location...but it’s always snoozing by nightfall.

Name: Grubbin
Bio: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.
Fact: Its strong jaw enables it to scrape trees and slurp out the sap. It normally lives underground.

Name: Charjabug
Bio: Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.
Fact: Its body is capable of storing electricity. On camping trips, people are grateful to have one around.

Name: Vikavolt
Bio: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (145) of all 84 Bug-type Pokémon.

Name: Crabrawler
Bio: Crabrawler has been known to mistake Exeggutor for a coconut tree and climb it. The enraged Exeggutor shakes it off and stomps it.
Fact: While guarding its weak points with its pincers, it looks for an opening and unleashes punches. When it loses, it foams at the mouth and faints.

Name: Crabominable
Bio: Before it stops to think, it just starts pummeling. There are records of its turning back avalanches with a flurry of punches.
Fact: The only Fighting/Ice type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Oricorio
Bio: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.
Fact: It beats its wings together to create fire. As it moves in the steps of its beautiful dance, it bathes opponents in intense flames.

Name: Cutiefly
Bio: An opponent’s aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent’s next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.
Fact: Tied for the smallest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 m.

Name: Ribombee
Bio: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.
Fact: It rolls up pollen into puffs. It makes many different varieties, some used as food and others used in battle.

Name: Rockruff
Bio: This Pokémon intimidates opponents by striking the ground with the rocks on its neck. The moment an opponent flinches, Rockruff attacks.
Fact: It’s considered to be a good Pokémon for beginners because of its friendliness, but its disposition grows rougher as it grows up.

Name: Lycanroc
Bio: With swift movements, this Pokémon gradually backs its prey into a corner. Lycanroc’s fangs are always aimed toward opponents’ weak spots.
Fact: Its quick movements confuse its enemies. Well equipped with claws and fangs, it also uses the sharp rocks in its mane as weapons.

Name: Wishiwashi
Bio: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.
Fact: When it’s in trouble, its eyes moisten and begin to shine. The shining light attracts its comrades, and they stand together against their enemies.

Name: Mareanie
Bio: Unlike their Alolan counterparts, the Mareanie of the Galar region have not yet figured out that the branches of Corsola are delicious.
Fact: It plunges the poison spike on its head into its prey. When the prey has weakened, Mareanie deals the finishing blow with its 10 tentacles.

Name: Toxapex
Bio: Within the poison sac in its body is a poison so toxic that Pokémon as large as Wailord will still be suffering three days after it first takes effect.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (152) of all 69 Poison-type Pokémon.

Name: Mudbray
Bio: It eats dirt to create mud and smears this mud all over its feet, giving them the grip needed to walk on rough terrain without slipping.
Fact: The mud stuck to Mudbray’s hooves enhances its grip and its powerful running gait.

Name: Mudsdale
Bio: Mudsdale has so much stamina that it could carry over 10 tons across the Galar region without rest or sleep.
Fact: It spits a mud that provides resistance to both wind and rain, so the walls of old houses were often coated with it.

Name: Dewpider
Bio: Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.
Fact: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

Name: Araquanid
Bio: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.
Fact: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

Name: Fomantis
Bio: During the day, Fomantis basks in sunlight and sleeps peacefully. It wakes and moves around at night.
Fact: During the day, it sleeps and soaks up light. When night falls, it walks around looking for a safer place to sleep.

Name: Lurantis
Bio: The petals on this Pokémon’s arms are thin and super sharp, and they can fire laser beams if Lurantis gathers light first.
Fact: It requires a lot of effort to maintain Lurantis’s vivid coloring, but some collectors enjoy this work and treat it as their hobby.

Name: Morelull
Bio: Morelull live in forests that stay dark even during the day. They scatter flickering spores that put enemies to sleep.
Fact: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.

Name: Shiinotic
Bio: If you see a light deep in a forest at night, don’t go near. Shiinotic will make you fall fast asleep.
Fact: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

Name: Salandit
Bio: This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.
Fact: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

Name: Salazzle
Bio: The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.
Fact: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

Name: Stufful
Bio: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.
Fact: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

Name: Bewear
Bio: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.
Fact: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

Name: Bounsweet
Bio: When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.
Fact: A delectable aroma pours from its body. They are often swallowed whole by Toucannon lured by that wafting deliciousness.

Name: Steenee
Bio: Any Corvisquire that pecks at this Pokémon will be greeted with a smack from its sepals followed by a sharp kick.
Fact: The sepals on its head developed to protect its body. These are quite hard, so even if pecked by bird Pokémon, this Pokémon is totally fine.

Name: Tsareena
Bio: A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon’s legs leaves a wound in the opponent’s body and soul that will never heal.
Fact: Its long, striking legs aren’t just for show but to be used to kick with skill. In victory, it shows off by kicking the defeated, laughing boisterously.

Name: Comfey
Bio: These Pokémon smell very nice. All Comfey wear different flowers, so each of these Pokémon has its own individual scent.
Fact: Tied for the smallest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 m.

Name: Oranguru
Bio: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.
Fact: Known for its extreme intelligence, this Pokémon will look down on inexperienced Trainers, so it’s best suited to veteran Trainers.

Name: Passimian
Bio: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.
Fact: They form groups of roughly 20 individuals. Their mutual bond is remarkable—they will never let down a comrade.

Name: Wimpod
Bio: Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy’s presence.
Fact: This Pokémon is a coward. As it desperately dashes off, the flailing of its many legs leaves a sparkling clean path in its wake.

Name: Golisopod
Bio: They live in sunken ships or in holes in the seabed. When Golisopod and Grapploct battle, the loser becomes the winner’s meal.
Fact: With a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater—or even air—right in two.

Name: Sandygast
Bio: Sandygast mainly inhabits beaches. It takes control of anyone who puts their hand into its mouth, forcing them to make its body bigger.
Fact: Born from a sand mound playfully built by a child, this Pokémon embodies the grudges of the departed.

Name: Palossand
FirstLocation: Loop Lagoon (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: This Pokémon lives on beaches, but it hates water. Palossand can’t maintain its castle-like shape if it gets drenched by a heavy rain.
Fact: Possessed people controlled by this Pokémon transformed its sand mound into a castle. As it evolved, its power to curse grew ever stronger.

Name: Pyukumuku
Bio: It’s covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.
Fact: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. It can eject its internal organs, which it uses to engulf its prey or battle enemies.

Name: Type: Null
Bio: It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.
Fact: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

Name: Silvally
Bio: The final factor needed to release this Pokémon’s true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.
Fact: Its trust in its partner is what awakens it. This Pokémon is capable of changing its type, a flexibility that is well displayed in battle.

Name: Minior
Bio: Although its outer shell is uncommonly durable, the shock of falling to the ground smashes the shell to smithereens.
Fact: Originally making its home in the ozone layer, it hurtles to the ground when the shell enclosing its body grows too heavy.

Name: Komala
Bio: It remains asleep from birth to death as a result of the sedative properties of the leaves that form its diet.
Fact: It is born asleep, and it dies asleep. All its movements are apparently no more than the results of it tossing and turning in its dreams.

Name: Turtonator
Bio: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (135) of all 56 Dragon-type Pokémon.

Name: Togedemaru
Bio: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.
Fact: The spiny fur on its back is normally at rest. When this Pokémon becomes agitated, its fur stands on end and stabs into its attackers.

Name: Mimikyu
Bio: There was a scientist who peeked under Mimikyu’s old rag in the name of research. The scientist died of a mysterious disease.
Fact: The only Ghost/Fairy type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Bruxish
Bio: Its skin is thick enough to fend off Mareanie’s spikes. With its robust teeth, Bruxish crunches up the spikes and eats them.
Fact: When it unleashes its psychic power from the protuberance on its head, the grating sound of grinding teeth echoes through the area.

Name: Drampa
Bio: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.
Fact: The only Normal/Dragon type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Dhelmise
Bio: After lowering its anchor, it waits for its prey. It catches large Wailord and drains their life-force.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (131) of all 51 Ghost-type Pokémon.

Name: Jangmo-o
Bio: Jangmo-o strikes its scales to communicate with others of its kind. Its scales are actually fur that’s become as hard as metal.
Fact: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

Name: Hakamo-o
Bio: Before attacking its enemies, it clashes its scales together and roars. Its sharp claws shred the opposition.
Fact: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

Name: Kommo-o
Bio: Certain ruins have paintings of ancient warriors wearing armor made of Kommo-o scales.
Fact: When it spots enemies, it threatens them by jingling the scales on its tail. Weak opponents will crack and flee in panic.

Name: Tapu Koko
Bio: The lightning-wielding guardian deity of Melemele, Tapu Koko is brimming with curiosity and appears before people from time to time.
Fact: This guardian deity of Melemele is brimming with curiosity. It summons thunderclouds and stores their lightning inside its body.

Name: Tapu Lele
Bio: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.
Fact: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

Name: Tapu Bulu
Bio: It makes ringing sounds with its tail to let others know where it is, avoiding unneeded conflicts. This guardian deity of Ula’ula controls plants.
Fact: It pulls large trees up by the roots and swings them around. It causes vegetation to grow, and then it absorbs energy from the growth.

Name: Tapu Fini
Bio: Although it’s called a guardian deity, terrible calamities sometimes befall those who recklessly approach Tapu Fini.
Fact: The dense fog it creates brings the downfall and destruction of its confused enemies. Ocean currents are the source of its energy.

Name: Cosmog
Bio: Cosmog is very curious but not very cautious, often placing itself in danger. If things start to look dicey, it teleports away.
Fact: Tied for the lightest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 kg.

Name: Cosmoem
Bio: It sucks in dust from the air at an astounding rate, frantically building up energy within its core as preparation for evolution.
Fact: The heaviest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 999.9 kg.

Name: Solgaleo
Bio: Solgaleo was once known as the Beast That Devours the Sun. Energy in the form of light radiates boundlessly from it.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (137) of all 54 Steel-type Pokémon.

Name: Lunala
Bio: It steals the light from its surroundings and then becomes the full moon, showering its own light across the night sky.
Fact: It is said to be a female evolution of Cosmog. When its third eye activates, away it flies to another world.

Name: Nihilego
Bio: It appeared in this world from an Ultra Wormhole. Nihilego appears to be a parasite that lives by feeding on people and Pokémon.
Fact: The only Rock/Poison type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Buzzwole
Bio: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (107) of all 84 Bug-type Pokémon.

Name: Pheromosa
Bio: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (137) of all 61 Fighting-type Pokémon.

Name: Xurkitree
Bio: They’ve been dubbed Ultra Beasts. Some of them stand unmoving, like trees, with their arms and legs stuck into the ground.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (173) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Celesteela
Bio: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.
Fact: The heaviest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 999.9 kg.

Name: Kartana
Bio: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (181) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Guzzlord
Bio: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.
Fact: Has the highest base HP (223) of all 55 Dark-type Pokémon.

Name: Necrozma
Bio: It needs light to survive, and it goes on a rampage seeking it out. Its laser beams will cut anything to pieces.
Fact: Reminiscent of the Ultra Beasts, this life-form, apparently asleep underground, is thought to have come from another world in ancient times.

Name: Magearna
Bio: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.
Fact: This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak.

Name: Marshadow
Bio: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.
Fact: The only Fighting/Ghost type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Poipole
Bio: An Ultra Beast that lives in a different world, it cackles wildly as it sprays its opponents with poison from the needles on its head.
Fact: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

Name: Naganadel
Bio: One kind of Ultra Beast, it fires a glowing, venomous liquid from its needles. This liquid is also immensely adhesive.
Fact: It stores hundreds of liters of poisonous liquid inside its body. It is one of the organisms known as UBs.

Name: Stakataka
Bio: When stone walls started moving and attacking, the brute’s true identity was this mysterious life-form, which brings to mind an Ultra Beast.
Fact: Has the highest base Defense (211) of all 54 Steel-type Pokémon.

Name: Blacephalon
Bio: A UB that appeared from an Ultra Wormhole, it causes explosions, then takes advantage of opponents’ surprise to rob them of their vitality.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (151) of all 71 Fire-type Pokémon.

Name: Zeraora
Bio: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.
Fact: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they’ll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

Name: Meltan
Bio: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.
Fact: It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.

Name: Melmetal
Bio: Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal’s heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.
Fact: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

Name: Grookey
Bio: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.
Fact: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.

Name: Thwackey
Bio: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.
Fact: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.

Name: Rillaboom
Bio: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.
Fact: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.

Name: Scorbunny
Bio: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it’s raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.
Fact: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon’s body. Once that happens, it’s ready to fight at full power.

Name: Raboot
Bio: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.
Fact: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.

Name: Cinderace
Bio: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.
Fact: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.

Name: Sobble
Bio: When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.
Fact: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won’t be able to resist weeping.

Name: Drizzile
Bio: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.
Fact: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

Name: Inteleon
Bio: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes’ weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.
Fact: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

Name: Skwovet
Bio: It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It’ll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.
Fact: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.

Name: Greedent
Bio: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.
Fact: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn’t notice the loss.

Name: Rookidee
Bio: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.
Fact: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.

Name: Corvisquire
Bio: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent’s strength.
Fact: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.

Name: Corviknight
Bio: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region’s airborne taxi service.
Fact: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.

Name: Blipbug
Bio: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.
Fact: A constant collector of information, this Pokémon is very smart. Very strong is what it isn’t.

Name: Dottler
Bio: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.
Fact: It barely moves, but it’s still alive. Hiding in its shell without food or water seems to have awakened its psychic powers.

Name: Orbeetle
Bio: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what’s around it—and what’s around it can include things over six miles away.
Fact: It’s famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.

Name: Nickit
Bio: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.
Fact: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.

Name: Thievul
Bio: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.
Fact: It secretly marks potential targets with a scent. By following the scent, it stalks its targets and steals from them when they least expect it.

Name: Gossifleur
Bio: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.
Fact: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.

Name: Eldegoss
Bio: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.
Fact: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.

Name: Wooloo
Bio: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.
Fact: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.

Name: Dubwool
Bio: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.
Fact: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You’ll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.

Name: Chewtle
Bio: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.
Fact: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

Name: Drednaw
Bio: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
Fact: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

Name: Yamper
Bio: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.
Fact: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.

Name: Boltund
Bio: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.
Fact: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.

Name: Rolycoly
Bio: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.
Fact: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.

Name: Carkol
Bio: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.
Fact: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.

Name: Coalossal
Bio: While it’s engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.
Fact: It’s usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Applin
Bio: As soon as it’s born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.
Fact: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it’s just an apple and nothing more.

Name: Flapple
FirstLocation: Stepping-Stone Sea (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.
Fact: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.

Name: Appletun
FirstLocation: Stepping-Stone Sea (The Isle Of Armor Shield)
Bio: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.
Fact: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.

Name: Silicobra
Bio: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.
Fact: As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.

Name: Sandaconda
Bio: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.
Fact: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.

Name: Cramorant
Bio: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.
Fact: It’s so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it’s battling midfight.

Name: Arrokuda
Bio: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.
Fact: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It’s very proud of that jaw.

Name: Barraskewda
Bio: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (136) of all 141 Water-type Pokémon.

Name: Toxel
Bio: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.
Fact: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.

Name: Toxtricity
Bio: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.
Fact: When this Pokémon sounds as if it’s strumming a guitar, it’s actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.

Name: Sizzlipede
Bio: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.
Fact: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.

Name: Centiskorch
Bio: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.
Fact: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

Name: Clobbopus
Bio: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.
Fact: It’s very curious, but its means of investigating things is to try to punch them with its tentacles. The search for food is what brings it onto land.

Name: Grapploct
Bio: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.
Fact: A body made up of nothing but muscle makes the grappling moves this Pokémon performs with its tentacles tremendously powerful.

Name: Sinistea
Bio: The teacup in which this Pokémon makes its home is a famous piece of antique tableware. Many forgeries are in circulation.
Fact: Tied for the smallest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 0.1 m.

Name: Polteageist
Bio: Leaving leftover black tea unattended is asking for this Pokémon to come along and pour itself into it, turning the tea into a new Polteageist.
Fact: This species lives in antique teapots. Most pots are forgeries, but on rare occasions, an authentic work is found.

Name: Hatenna
Bio: If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.
Fact: Via the protrusion on its head, it senses other creatures’ emotions. If you don’t have a calm disposition, it will never warm up to you.

Name: Hattrem
Bio: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.
Fact: No matter who you are, if you bring strong emotions near this Pokémon, it will silence you violently.

Name: Hatterene
Bio: If you’re too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.
Fact: Has the highest base Sp. Attack (136) of all 52 Fairy-type Pokémon.

Name: Impidimp
Bio: It sneaks into people’s homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.
Fact: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

Name: Morgrem
Bio: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.
Fact: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Bio: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.
Fact: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

Name: Obstagoon
Bio: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.
Fact: The only Dark/Normal type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Perrserker
Bio: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.
Fact: What appears to be an iron helmet is actually hardened hair. This Pokémon lives for the thrill of battle.

Name: Cursola
Bio: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You’ll become stiff as stone if you touch it.
Fact: Its shell is overflowing with its heightened otherworldly energy. The ectoplasm serves as protection for this Pokémon’s core spirit.

Name: Sirfetch'd
Bio: After deflecting attacks with its hard leaf shield, it strikes back with its sharp leek stalk. The leek stalk is both weapon and food.
Fact: Only Farfetch’d that have survived many battles can attain this evolution. When this Pokémon’s leek withers, it will retire from combat.

Name: Mr. Rime
Bio: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.
Fact: It’s highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

Name: Runerigus
Bio: Never touch its shadowlike body, or you’ll be shown the horrific memories behind the picture carved into it.
Fact: A powerful curse was woven into an ancient painting. After absorbing the spirit of a Yamask, the painting began to move.

Name: Milcery
Bio: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.
Fact: This Pokémon was born from sweet-smelling particles in the air. Its body is made of cream.

Name: Alcremie
Bio: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.
Fact: When it trusts a Trainer, it will treat them to berries it’s decorated with cream.

Name: Falinks
Bio: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.
Fact: Five of them are troopers, and one is the brass. The brass’s orders are absolute.

Name: Pincurchin
Bio: It stores electricity in each spine. Even if one gets broken off, it still continues to emit electricity for at least three hours.
Fact: It feeds on seaweed, using its teeth to scrape it off rocks. Electric current flows from the tips of its spines.

Name: Snom
Bio: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.
Fact: It spits out thread imbued with a frigid sort of energy and uses it to tie its body to branches, disguising itself as an icicle while it sleeps.

Name: Frosmoth
Bio: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.
Fact: Icy scales fall from its wings like snow as it flies over fields and mountains. The temperature of its wings is less than −290 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Stonjourner
Bio: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.
Fact: It stands in grasslands, watching the sun’s descent from zenith to horizon. This Pokémon has a talent for delivering dynamic kicks.

Name: Eiscue
Bio: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.
Fact: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

Name: Indeedee
Bio: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.
Fact: It uses the horns on its head to sense the emotions of others. Males will act as valets for those they serve, looking after their every need.

Name: Morpeko
Bio: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they’re precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.
Fact: The only Electric/Dark type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Cufant
Bio: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.
Fact: It digs up the ground with its trunk. It’s also very strong, being able to carry loads of over five tons without any problem at all.

Name: Copperajah
Bio: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.
Fact: They came over from another region long ago and worked together with humans. Their green skin is resistant to water.

Name: Dracozolt
Bio: The powerful muscles in its tail generate its electricity. Compared to its lower body, its upper half is entirely too small.
Fact: In ancient times, it was unbeatable thanks to its powerful lower body, but it went extinct anyway after it depleted all its plant-based food sources.

Name: Arctozolt
Bio: This Pokémon lived on prehistoric seashores and was able to preserve food with the ice on its body. It went extinct because it moved so slowly.
Fact: The only Electric/Ice type in the entire Pokédex.

Name: Dracovish
