Bio: Its mighty legs are capable of running at speeds exceeding 40 mph, but this Pokémon can’t breathe unless it’s underwater.
Fact: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

Name: Arctovish
Bio: The skin on its face is impervious to attack, but breathing difficulties made this Pokémon go extinct anyway.
Fact: Though it’s able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

Name: Duraludon
Bio: The special metal that composes its body is very light, so this Pokémon has considerable agility. It lives in caves because it dislikes the rain.
Fact: Its body resembles polished metal, and it’s both lightweight and strong. The only drawback is that it rusts easily.

Name: Dreepy
Bio: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.
Fact: After being reborn as a ghost Pokémon, Dreepy wanders the areas it used to inhabit back when it was alive in prehistoric seas.

Name: Drakloak
Bio: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it’ll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.
Fact: It’s capable of flying faster than 120 mph. It battles alongside Dreepy and dotes on them until they successfully evolve.

Name: Dragapult
Bio: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult’s horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (142) of all 56 Dragon-type Pokémon.

Name: Zacian
Bio: This Pokémon has slumbered for many years. Some say it’s Zamazenta’s elder sister—others say the two Pokémon are rivals.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (138) of all 52 Fairy-type Pokémon.

Name: Zamazenta
Bio: This Pokémon slept for aeons while in the form of a statue. It was asleep for so long, people forgot that it ever existed.
Fact: Has the highest base total base stats (660) of all 61 Fighting-type Pokémon.

Name: Eternatus
Bio: It was inside a meteorite that fell 20,000 years ago. There seems to be a connection between this Pokémon and the Dynamax phenomenon.
Fact: The tallest Pokémon in the Pokédex at 20 m.

Name: Kubfu
FirstLocation: Master Dojo (The Isle Of Armor Sword)
Bio: If Kubfu pulls the long white hair on its head, its fighting spirit heightens and power wells up from the depths of its belly.
Fact: Kubfu trains hard to perfect its moves. The moves it masters will determine which form it takes when it evolves.

Name: Urshifu
Bio: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.
Fact: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in the one-hit KO. Its strategy is to leap in close to foes and land a devastating blow with a hardened fist.

Name: Zarude
Bio: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.
Fact: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It’s incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.

Name: Regieleki
Bio: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar’s electricity.
Fact: Has the highest base Speed (200) of all 898 Pokémon.

Name: Regidrago
Bio: Its body is composed of crystallized dragon energy. Regidrago is said to have the powers of every dragon Pokémon.
Fact: An academic theory proposes that Regidrago’s arms were once the head of an ancient dragon Pokémon. The theory remains unproven.

Name: Glastrier
Bio: Glastrier has tremendous physical strength, and the mask of ice covering its face is 100 times harder than diamond.
Fact: Has the highest base Attack (145) of all 41 Ice-type Pokémon.

Name: Spectrier
Bio: As it dashes through the night, Spectrier absorbs the life-force of sleeping creatures. It craves silence and solitude.
Fact: It probes its surroundings with all its senses save one—it doesn’t use its sense of sight. Spectrier’s kicks are said to separate soul from body.

Name: Calyrex
Bio: Calyrex is known in legend as a king that ruled over Galar in ancient times. It has the power to cause hearts to mend and plants to spring forth.
Fact: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.
